title: Startup optional capability handling regression (missing prepare tool)
description:
## Summary
Current runtime still uses legacy Home Manager activation behavior in startup paths, which can fail hard when expected tooling is absent. Under the environment-interface contract, this maps to optional startup capability handling and should be resolved by contract-driven lifecycle implementation.

## Current behavior
- Startup can fail non-zero when activation tooling is unavailable in the selected env/shell context.
- Failure surfaces as command-scoped startup error and blocks `havn up` completion.

## Expected behavior
- Optional startup capability absence should be non-fatal (with concise status signal).
- Executed capability failures remain fatal with actionable command-scoped error framing.
- Behavior should match `specs/environment-interface.md` and linked runtime tasks.

## Tracking
- Superseded implementation path is tracked under `havn-xl3` tasks.
- This bug remains as regression tracker and is intentionally dependency-blocked to avoid direct, out-of-sequence fixes from `bd ready`.
notes:
Workflow note: dependency-blocked behind havn-xl3.1/.2/.4 so agents do not pick this as ready work before contract-runtime changes land.
Close condition: close this bug only after havn-xl3.1, havn-xl3.2, and havn-xl3.4 are completed and runtime behavior is verified against specs/environment-interface.md (missing optional capability is non-fatal with status signal; executed capability failure remains fatal with actionable error).
Verified close conditions after claiming: havn-xl3.1, havn-xl3.2, and havn-xl3.4 are closed; runtime behavior matches specs/environment-interface.md in tests. Ran go test ./internal/container ./internal/cli -run 'Startup|StartOrAttach|ContractMatrix' and confirmed missing optional havn-session-prepare is non-fatal with status signal while executed capability failures are fatal and actionable.

title: Add coverage for Home Manager session matrix
description: Add tests covering command-surface behavior (havn [path], havn up [path], havn enter [path]) for Home Manager lifecycle integration and compatibility with ad-hoc nix develop usage.
acceptance_criteria:
- Tests cover root startup activation expectations.
- Tests cover havn up [path] non-interactive behavior with lifecycle integration.
- Tests cover havn enter [path] plain-shell semantics plus manual path expectations.
- Tests confirm no regression to existing startup/entry contracts.
notes: Added CLI command-surface coverage for Home Manager lifecycle matrix: root startup aborts before shell handoff on startup errors, up remains command-scoped/non-interactive on lifecycle failures, and enter contract tests now assert plain-shell/no-startup-overrides guidance.

title: Add Home Manager activation to primary startup flow
description: Implement Home Manager lifecycle activation in the primary interactive startup path so havn [path] presents a session with user configuration applied before shell handoff.
acceptance_criteria:
- havn [path] performs Home Manager activation in the startup/attach flow.
- Activation runs before handing control to the interactive shell.
- User-facing errors are actionable and identify Home Manager activation failures.
- Existing startup behavior (including nix registry preparation and shell exit-code propagation) remains intact.
notes:
Decision: activation failures in havn [path] are blocking errors (hard-fail) with actionable guidance.
Implemented Home Manager activation in root startup attach flow: after nix registry prep and before interactive shell handoff, startup now runs 'nix develop <env>#<shell> -c home-manager switch --flake <env>' inside the project container. Activation errors now surface as blocking startup failures with actionable guidance to use 'havn enter <path>' for manual debugging; no-attach mode remains unchanged.

title: Preserve non-interactive Home Manager behavior for havn up
description: Ensure Home Manager lifecycle integration does not violate the no-attach contract for havn up [path] while keeping lifecycle behavior consistent with the startup model.
acceptance_criteria:
- havn up [path] remains non-interactive and never drops into a shell.
- Any Home Manager-related startup behavior for havn up [path] is non-interactive-safe.
- Error handling for havn up [path] remains actionable and command-scoped (havn up:).
- Existing successful status output remains compatible.
notes: Updated startup orchestration so Home Manager activation now runs for no-attach mode across running, stopped, and newly created containers. Added/updated container startup tests to assert activation occurs without interactive attach, preserving non-interactive up behavior while failing with actionable command-scoped errors.

title: Provide manual Home Manager activation path for enter sessions
description: Keep havn enter [path] as plain-shell entry while adding a low-friction, documented manual Home Manager activation path available from entered sessions.
acceptance_criteria:
- havn enter [path] still enters plain bash without implicit nix develop.
- Users have a clear manual Home Manager activation path from enter sessions.
- Guidance is visible in user docs and/or command UX where appropriate.
- Missing/stopped-container actionable errors remain unchanged.
notes: Added explicit manual Home Manager activation guidance for enter sessions in CLI help and user docs, including the canonical nix develop + home-manager switch command with <env>/<shell> placeholders.

title: Specify Home Manager session lifecycle contract
description: Define the precise behavioral contract for Home Manager lifecycle integration across havn [path], havn up [path], and havn enter [path], including activation timing, error handling, and user-facing messaging. Tighten planned direction into implementation-ready semantics while preserving ad-hoc nix develop workflows.
acceptance_criteria:
- Spec text defines exact activation points for each command surface.
- Failure behavior is explicit (what blocks entry vs what degrades with guidance).
- Contract preserves non-interactive behavior for havn up [path].
- Contract preserves plain-shell semantics for havn enter [path] with a manual path.
- Contract explicitly states compatibility expectations for ad-hoc nix develop.
notes:
Decision: Home Manager activation failure during havn [path] should hard-fail startup with actionable error; do not hand off to shell on activation failure.
Specified Home Manager lifecycle contract across havn/havn up/havn enter: startup commands run activation before completion/handoff; havn is fail-closed on activation failure; up stays non-interactive and fails non-zero on activation failure; enter remains plain bash with manual activation path guidance; ad-hoc nix develop compatibility explicitly preserved.

title: Integrate Home Manager lifecycle into havn sessions
description: havn sessions can start with dev shell tooling available but without Home Manager user config applied, which creates a split environment. Make havn the primary lifecycle orchestrator so full user config can be activated predictably (automatic for primary flows and easy manual activation for bare enter), while preserving ad-hoc nix develop workflows.
acceptance_criteria:
- Primary interactive startup (`havn [path]`) activates Home Manager user configuration before handing control to the session shell.
- `havn enter [path]` remains plain `bash` entry and includes a documented, low-friction manual Home Manager activation path.
- `havn up [path]` remains non-interactive; Home Manager lifecycle handling must preserve the no-attach contract.
- Ad-hoc `nix develop` workflows continue to work from entered sessions and are not blocked by Home Manager lifecycle orchestration.
- Authoritative specs capture this behavior and derivative docs can reference the updated contract.
notes: Clarification captured: Home Manager activation failure on primary startup is hard-fail with actionable error.

title: Handle chown-restricted nix registry prep
description: Regression after 74d04b3: havn up fails when chown on /home/devuser/.local/state/nix/registry.json returns operation not permitted in some runtimes. Runtime prep should tolerate chown failure only when registry remains writable by devuser, and still fail when not writable.

title: havn build fails when host GID already exists in base image
description: havn auto-build of havn-base:latest fails during Dockerfile user/group creation when gid 1000 already exists in ubuntu:24.04. Repro: docker build --build-arg UID=1000 --build-arg GID=1000 -t havn-base:latest docker/. Failing step: groupadd --gid 1000 devuser && useradd --uid 1000 --gid 1000 --create-home --shell /bin/bash devuser. Error: groupadd reports GID 1000 already exists and build exits with code 4. Expected behavior: build should handle existing uid/gid idempotently by reusing or remapping account/group instead of failing.
close_reason: Fixed base image build to handle existing UID/GID collisions; verified with docker build and make check

title: Fail invalid environment overrides instead of silently ignoring them
description:
Malformed env overrides such as non-numeric `HAVN_CPUS` currently disappear silently. This task makes env-sourced overrides uphold the same trustworthiness as file- and flag-sourced config by failing invalid effective config instead of weakening precedence fidelity.

## Scope
- detect malformed env override values during effective config resolution
- surface invalid env overrides as user-visible config errors

## Specs
- specs/configuration.md §215

## Wires up
- more trustworthy env override precedence
- fewer hidden startup surprises

## Acceptance
- malformed env overrides do not silently fall back
- invalid env override values fail effective config resolution with actionable errors
notes: Implemented strict env override parsing for HAVN_CPUS: malformed values now fail effective config resolution with config_validation_error (field env.HAVN_CPUS) instead of silently falling back.

title: Tighten startup config validation for dependable startup
description:
The core startup path is mostly wired, but invalid effective config can still slip through too far or silently degrade precedence behavior. This epic makes startup fail early and deterministically when the resolved startup contract is invalid.

## Scope
- validate merged startup port values by contract, not just syntax
- fail malformed env overrides instead of silently ignoring them
- resolve the config-mount scope mismatch between runtime and published configuration contract

## Out of scope
- unrelated configuration redesign
- adding new startup flags or precedence layers

## Specs
- specs/configuration.md §141
- specs/configuration.md §150
- specs/configuration.md §215

## Wires up
- startup that rejects invalid effective config before runtime side effects
- clearer, more trustworthy override semantics

## Acceptance
- invalid merged port values are rejected before container creation
- malformed env overrides do not silently fall back
- config-mount scope is consistent between published contract and implementation

title: Make the personal Dolt-enabled workflow usable and trustworthy
description:
Your real minimum working version is a personal single-user workflow where beads/shared Dolt is present in every project. This epic hardens the smallest honest slice needed to make that workflow usable and trustworthy without broadening scope into team or platform concerns.

## Scope
- make Dolt-enabled startup and diagnostics trustworthy for personal use
- tighten startup validation and override fidelity that affect every project
- add missing CLI-boundary proof for the root startup path you actually use
- fix the most important shared-Dolt migration and status fidelity gaps that affect personal use

## Out of scope
- collaborator or team workflow hardening beyond the personal baseline
- broad doctor redesign unrelated to startup or shared-Dolt truthfulness
- optional auth/TLS/shared-Dolt security expansions not required by the current personal workflow
acceptance_criteria:
- a personal Dolt-enabled project can start and attach with trustworthy effective config, shared-Dolt setup, and injected beads env
- doctor is trustworthy enough to troubleshoot common config and Dolt-state problems in the personal workflow
- remaining migration and status behavior used in the personal workflow is either trustworthy or explicitly narrowed in the published contract

title: Validate shared Dolt database identifiers at the execution boundary
description:
Make shared-Dolt command and startup paths enforce the intended safety guarantees for database identifiers before interpolating them into SQL-oriented execution paths.

## Scope
- shared-Dolt database-name validation at startup and direct command paths
- alignment between config validation and Dolt execution-boundary expectations
- rejection of unsafe or unsupported database identifiers before execution

## Specs
- specs/shared-dolt-server.md
- specs/configuration.md

## Wires up
- safer shared-Dolt execution behavior for database provisioning and direct database operations

## Acceptance
- shared-Dolt paths do not rely on unchecked database identifiers at the execution boundary
- startup and direct shared-Dolt commands apply consistent identifier-safety rules
- database-oriented operations fail safely before execution when identifiers violate the contract
notes: Implemented execution-boundary validation for shared-Dolt database identifiers before SQL interpolation. Added centralized identifier validator and enforced it in startup provisioning (CREATE DATABASE), direct drop, import, and export paths. Added regression tests for unsafe identifiers to ensure operations fail before backend exec/copy.

title: Close remaining CLI contract gaps outside core effective-config flow
description:
Close the remaining user-visible CLI contract mismatches that were identified in the audit but are not yet captured by the current config/runtime tasks. This is for command behavior gaps adjacent to, but not identical with, core effective-config assembly.

## Scope
- `havn build` build-time `--image` override behavior
- `havn list` filtering to the intended running project-container set
- `HAVN_SSH_PORT` contract alignment with the documented single-port form
- `havn stop --all --json` partial-failure result semantics

## Specs
- specs/cli-framework.md
- specs/configuration.md
- specs/havn-overview.md

## Wires up
- tighter user-visible CLI contract fidelity outside the main startup config path

## Acceptance
- the listed CLI surfaces match their published contract behavior
- JSON and human outcomes for the affected commands no longer overstate success or expose unintended container sets
- environment and flag semantics for these surfaces align with the documented contract
notes: Implemented CLI contract fixes: build now accepts --image override, list only returns running project containers (excluding havn-dolt), HAVN_SSH_PORT now accepts single host port and derives :22 mapping, and stop --all --json now reports partial failure with status=error and exit code 1.

title: Fix startup recovery, error classification, and containment gaps
description:
Bring startup recovery and containment behavior into line with the runtime contract by fixing failure classification, stopped-container recovery, and config-mount trust boundaries that remain outside the current ports/mount-mode task.

## Scope
- startup classification of network and volume inspect failures
- startup handling of `sshd` init failures
- stopped-container recovery behavior before container creation
- containment rules for absolute config mounts and resolved in-container targets

## Specs
- specs/havn-overview.md
- specs/base-image.md
- specs/configuration.md

## Wires up
- safer and more predictable startup/runtime behavior under failure and mount-edge cases

## Acceptance
- startup distinguishes missing resources from other inspect failures where the contract requires it
- startup follows the intended recovery/abort behavior for stopped containers and `sshd` init failures
- config-mount behavior no longer broadens host/container trust beyond the published containment intent
notes: Implemented startup contract fixes: stopped project containers are now started (not recreated) before attach; sshd post-start init failures now abort startup with context; network and volume inspect paths now distinguish not-found from other inspect failures; config mount resolution now rejects matches outside home-directory containment. Added/updated unit tests in internal/container/start_test.go, internal/volume/manager_test.go, and internal/mount/resolve_test.go to cover each behavior.

title: Clean up doctor output and exit contract behavior
description:
Make doctor’s CLI behavior match its published reporting contract when checks warn, fail, or are skipped. This focuses on output discipline and report completeness, not the underlying checks themselves.

## Scope
- warn/error reporting behavior after the doctor report is emitted
- separation between doctor report output and generic CLI error formatting
- informational handling of no-container tier skipping
- consistency of human and JSON output behavior for doctor-specific outcomes

## Specs
- specs/havn-doctor.md
- specs/cli-framework.md

## Wires up
- doctor-specific output and exit behavior that matches the published contract

## Acceptance
- doctor does not emit extra generic error payloads/messages after already printing its report
- doctor preserves the documented exit-code behavior while keeping output contract-consistent
- no-container tier skipping is surfaced in the doctor report the way the contract expects

title: Make doctor use effective project config and validation semantics
description:
Make `havn doctor` build its checks from the same effective project configuration semantics that startup uses, rather than defaults and raw file parsing. This covers command-scoped config loading, merged validation behavior, and doctor’s config-related checks.

## Scope
- doctor-side effective config loading
- command-scoped global config selection behavior
- merged config validation semantics used by doctor checks
- config-related doctor checks that currently inspect raw files instead of effective state

## Specs
- specs/havn-doctor.md
- specs/configuration.md
- specs/cli-framework.md

## Wires up
- doctor checks built from effective project state rather than defaults

## Acceptance
- doctor uses the intended effective config semantics for the current command
- doctor config checks reflect merged/validated project state rather than only raw file presence
- doctor does not diverge from startup on config-related expectations for the same project
notes:
Doctor now resolves effective config with command-scoped global config () and startup-aligned merge/flake/default/validation semantics before constructing checks. Host checks now consume the same global config path as invocation context, and project_config reports merged-value validation failures instead of only raw parse/file presence. Added coverage for config-flag usage in doctor CLI and project-config validation error reporting in doctor host checks.
Doctor now resolves effective config with command-scoped global config (--config) using startup-aligned merge, flake resolution, derived defaults, and validation semantics before constructing checks. Host checks use the invoked global config path, and project_config now reports merged-value validation failures in addition to parse/file checks. Added tests covering --config behavior in doctor CLI and validation-error reporting in doctor host checks.

title: Align doctor project and container targeting with startup identity
description:
Make doctor select and interpret project containers using the same identity rules as startup. This covers default doctor targeting, `--all` behavior, and per-container project context.

## Scope
- default project-container targeting
- shared identity rules between startup and doctor
- `doctor --all` scoping to running havn-managed project containers
- per-container project context instead of reusing current-directory state for all targets

## Specs
- specs/havn-doctor.md
- specs/configuration.md

## Wires up
- correct doctor targeting for project containers and per-project context

## Acceptance
- default doctor targeting uses the same project/container identity expectations as startup
- `doctor --all` is limited to the intended project-container scope
- per-container checks use the correct project context rather than current-directory assumptions

title: Verify resolved mounts and SSH forwarding in doctor
description:
Bring doctor’s container-side mount and SSH checks into parity with actual runtime wiring. This closes the gap where doctor checks unresolved config entries or probes the wrong in-container socket path.

## Scope
- project mount verification against effective runtime mount paths
- config-mount verification against resolved mount targets
- mount access-mode fidelity in doctor checks where the contract expects it
- SSH-agent verification against the actual forwarded runtime socket/functionality

## Specs
- specs/havn-doctor.md
- specs/configuration.md

## Wires up
- doctor runtime checks that reflect the same container wiring startup produces

## Acceptance
- doctor mount checks verify the resolved runtime-mounted paths the container actually uses
- doctor SSH-agent checks verify the effective forwarded socket/functionality rather than a literal placeholder path
- doctor runtime checks no longer rely on unresolved config strings where the contract expects effective state

title: Harden import and export safety semantics
description:
Bring import/export behavior into line with the shared-Dolt safety contract for overwrite handling, verification, and user-visible result semantics so persistent data operations are safer and clearer.

## Scope
- import overwrite/force semantics
- identity-mismatch handling and reporting
- export destination verification
- result reporting for safety-relevant import/export outcomes

## Specs
- specs/shared-dolt-server.md

## Wires up
- safer persistent-data migration flows for shared Dolt

## Acceptance
- import and export make safety-relevant outcomes explicit to users
- overwrite behavior and mismatch reporting match the shared-Dolt contract
- export does not report success without the required destination-side verification
notes: Implemented shared-Dolt import/export safety semantics: export now verifies destination contains the requested database before reporting success, and import now reports explicit overwrite outcomes (status line + JSON overrode flag) when --force replaces an existing database. Covered by new red/green tests in internal/dolt/migrate_test.go and internal/cli/dolt_test.go. Commit: c4c413c

title: Enforce shared Dolt ownership and lifecycle checks across commands
description:
Make all `havn dolt` command paths consistently enforce the shared-server ownership and lifecycle contract, rather than relying on partial checks only in startup.

## Scope
- ownership validation across direct shared-Dolt command surfaces
- lifecycle preconditions for commands that inspect or mutate shared-Dolt state
- consistent treatment of unmanaged or conflicting `havn-dolt` containers
- command-path alignment for stop, database operations, connectivity, and export/import interactions

## Specs
- specs/shared-dolt-server.md

## Wires up
- one coherent ownership/lifecycle contract across shared-Dolt commands

## Acceptance
- direct shared-Dolt commands do not operate on unmanaged/conflicting containers as if they were havn-managed
- shared-Dolt command behavior is consistent about lifecycle preconditions and ownership enforcement
- users get one coherent contract across startup and direct Dolt commands
notes: Implemented shared Dolt ownership/lifecycle enforcement across direct commands by adding running+ownership preconditions for stop, databases, drop, connect, import, and export. Added explicit ServerNotRunningError plus CLI formatting guidance, and expanded unit/CLI tests for unmanaged/conflicting havn-dolt and non-running lifecycle cases.

title: Align shared Dolt command config and database defaulting
description:
Make shared-Dolt command paths use the intended command-side config behavior needed for safe operation, and ensure project database defaulting is consistent where shared-Dolt flows depend on it.

## Scope
- command-side Dolt config loading needed by `havn dolt` operations
- consistency of shared-Dolt image/port/network behavior where commands rely on config
- project database defaulting for shared-Dolt startup and related flows
- consistency between startup and direct shared-Dolt operations for database selection

## Specs
- specs/shared-dolt-server.md
- specs/configuration.md

## Wires up
- consistent shared-Dolt behavior across startup, start, import, export, and related command flows

## Acceptance
- shared-Dolt commands use the intended config behavior for safe operation
- project database defaulting is consistent where the shared-Dolt contract requires it
- startup and direct shared-Dolt operations do not diverge on database selection behavior

title: Wire migration detection into shared Dolt user flows
description:
Connect the existing migration-detection capability to the user-visible shared-Dolt flows where the spec expects migration guidance, so users are warned when local beads data is present.

## Scope
- production use of migration detection
- user-visible migration notice behavior in shared-Dolt startup or related flows
- flow alignment between migration detection and project shared-Dolt setup

## Specs
- specs/shared-dolt-server.md

## Wires up
- migration-awareness in shared-Dolt startup and onboarding flows

## Acceptance
- migration detection is used in production shared-Dolt flows rather than only in tests
- users see the intended migration guidance when local beads data is present
- migration messaging appears in the flow where the shared-Dolt contract expects it
notes: Wired migration detection into startup flow: container startup now calls Dolt setup migration notice after EnsureReady, and emits a user-facing import hint when local .beads/dolt/<db> exists and shared DB is absent. Added tests in internal/container/start_test.go and internal/dolt/setup_test.go for notice emission and hint formatting.

title: Fix first-run shared Dolt server startup and readiness flow
description:
Make the shared-server startup path coherent for first-run and reused-server cases so `havn` can reliably ensure `havn-dolt` exists, starts correctly, and is ready before project database work begins.

## Scope
- first-run shared-server creation flow
- startup sequencing around container creation, config seeding, and server start
- readiness behavior for newly created, restarted, and already-running shared servers
- startup contract fidelity for shared-server availability before database provisioning

## Specs
- specs/shared-dolt-server.md

## Wires up
- reliable shared-Dolt availability for startup and direct `havn dolt start`

## Acceptance
- first-run shared-server startup follows a viable lifecycle
- reused running shared servers are readiness-checked before database work
- startup does not proceed to project database provisioning until the shared server is ready
notes: Implemented shared-Dolt startup sequencing fixes: (1) running managed servers now go through readiness probe (SELECT 1) before DB provisioning, and (2) first-run path now creates container before seeding /etc/dolt/servercfg.d then starts and probes readiness. Updated unit tests for readiness-on-reuse and creation/copy ordering.

title: Make havn config show match effective-config contract
description:
Bring `havn config show` into parity with the effective-config contract and startup semantics so inspection is trustworthy. This covers both the resolved values shown and the published provenance contract.

## Scope
- command behavior parity between startup config resolution and `config show`
- support for the correct global-config selection behavior
- human and JSON output parity with the documented config-inspection contract
- provenance coverage for documented fields, including nested source metadata

## Specs
- specs/configuration.md
- specs/cli-framework.md

## Wires up
- trustworthy config inspection for the same effective state startup uses

## Acceptance
- `havn config show` reflects the same effective config semantics used by startup
- JSON output matches the published config-inspection contract
- provenance/source metadata is present for the documented fields and structure
notes: Implemented config-show effective-resolution parity with startup semantics: honors --config global path, uses metadata-aware merge, resolves project flake fallback (path:./.havn), validates merged config, includes [environment] in output, and emits nested source provenance for resources/dolt per contract. Added/updated CLI tests for nested source, --config behavior, and environment output.

title: Align config validation and environment inspection semantics
description:
Make config validation and config inspection treat environment-driven settings consistently with the published contract. This closes the gap where `[environment]` behavior is partly enforced late in startup and not surfaced in inspection.

## Scope
- validation semantics for environment passthrough entries
- reserved-key and unset-host-variable handling where the config contract requires it
- effective inspection of `[environment]` in config output
- consistency between validation-time and startup-time treatment of environment config

## Specs
- specs/configuration.md

## Wires up
- consistent environment-related config behavior across validation, inspection, and startup

## Acceptance
- environment-related config rules are enforced consistently with the published contract
- config inspection includes the documented environment portion of effective config
- users do not get contradictory results between config inspection and startup behavior
notes: Implemented config-layer environment resolution/validation parity: added internal/config.ResolveProjectEnvironment and invoked it from config.Validate so reserved keys (SSH_AUTH_SOCK, BEADS_DOLT_*) and unset  passthroughs fail consistently in both startup and config show. Updated container startup to reuse config resolver instead of duplicating logic; added validation and CLI tests for these cases.

title: Fix startup effective-config precedence and defaults
description:
Make startup honor the published effective-config contract for precedence-sensitive fields and derived defaults. This covers the gaps where config is parsed but not merged or defaulted according to the configuration spec.

## Scope
- boolean precedence for `mounts.ssh.forward_agent`, `mounts.ssh.authorized_keys`, and `dolt.enabled`
- startup-side config/default behavior that should be resolved before runtime consumes config
- derived defaults such as `dolt.database` when Dolt is enabled
- command-scoped effective-config fidelity for startup

## Specs
- specs/configuration.md
- specs/cli-framework.md

## Wires up
- correct effective config for root-command startup

## Acceptance
- startup resolves config using the documented precedence rules for booleans and derived defaults
- explicit higher-precedence `false` values win where the spec says they should
- startup gets a stable effective `dolt.database` when the spec says one should exist
notes: Implemented startup boolean precedence fix by tracking explicit boolean presence in config file metadata and resolving startup config with ResolveWithMetadata. This makes explicit project/global false values for mounts.ssh.forward_agent, mounts.ssh.authorized_keys, and dolt.enabled override lower-precedence true values while preserving existing defaulting (including dolt.database derivation in root startup). Added regression coverage in internal/cli/root_test.go.

title: Apply effective ports and mount modes at runtime
description:
Ensure the effective runtime config is actually enforced when containers are created. This closes the gap where resolved ports and mount-mode intent exist in config but are dropped before Docker sees them.

## Scope
- effective publish-set application during container creation
- SSH `--port` contribution to runtime behavior
- host-port availability enforcement for requested publishes
- mount read-only/read-write fidelity from resolution through Docker translation

## Specs
- specs/configuration.md
- specs/havn-overview.md
- specs/base-image.md

## Wires up
- runtime container behavior that matches resolved networking and mount config

## Acceptance
- requested effective ports are published at runtime
- startup fails when requested host ports are unavailable
- resolved mount mode intent is preserved through runtime container creation
notes: Implemented runtime enforcement for effective publish set and mount modes. Startup now checks host-port availability before container creation, passes effective ports through to Docker port bindings/exposed ports, and preserves bind read-only/read-write flags end-to-end from mount resolution to Docker mounts. Added unit coverage in container/docker packages for effective ports, mount mode fidelity, and startup failure on unavailable host ports. Note: pre-commit lint warning about unused dolt helper is tracked separately in havn-db0.

title: Harden shared Dolt lifecycle and data safety
description:
Stabilize shared Dolt as a safe, trustworthy subsystem by fixing lifecycle sequencing, defaulting, ownership/readiness enforcement, and migration safety. The goal is to treat shared Dolt as persistent data infrastructure, not just a command surface.

## Scope
- shared-server startup and readiness behavior
- ownership and lifecycle enforcement across commands
- database defaulting and migration detection
- import/export safety and overwrite semantics

## Specs
- specs/shared-dolt-server.md
- specs/configuration.md

## Wires up
- reliable shared Dolt setup for project startup and direct `havn dolt` commands

## Acceptance
- shared Dolt commands and startup follow one coherent lifecycle contract
- database selection/defaulting is reliable
- migration and import/export flows protect persistent data from partial or unsafe writes

title: Make havn doctor trustworthy against effective state
description:
Refit `havn doctor` so it inspects the same effective project state that startup uses, making its human and JSON output reliable for troubleshooting. This epic focuses on correctness of checks, project targeting, and output behavior.

## Scope
- effective config loading for doctor
- project/container identity matching
- resolved mount and SSH-agent verification
- output and exit behavior consistency

## Specs
- specs/havn-doctor.md
- specs/configuration.md
- specs/cli-framework.md

## Wires up
- shared effective-state primitives between startup and doctor

## Acceptance
- doctor checks run against effective runtime state rather than defaults
- doctor targets the correct project/container context
- doctor output matches the published contract without extra generic error noise

title: Align effective config with runtime behavior
description:
Bring the effective-config pipeline into parity with actual runtime behavior so the values `havn` resolves are the values startup uses and users can inspect. This is the highest-leverage roadmap item because it affects startup, `config show`, later `doctor` fidelity, and several documented CLI guarantees.

## Scope
- effective config resolution and runtime application
- runtime flag/env/config precedence fidelity
- config inspection parity with startup
- ports, mounts, booleans, defaults, and related translation gaps

## Specs
- specs/configuration.md
- specs/cli-framework.md
- specs/havn-overview.md

## Wires up
- shared effective-state flow for startup and config inspection

## Acceptance
- startup behavior matches resolved effective config
- `havn config show` reflects the same effective state startup uses
- documented runtime config behaviors are enforced rather than only resolved

title: Resolve should merge volume and dolt config fields
description: While wiring havn volume list, discovered config.Resolve does not merge [volumes] fields (and does not set dolt values), so effective config can ignore configured volume names. This breaks volume list output for customized volume names.
notes: Fixed by merging [volumes] and dolt fields in config.Resolve applyConfig; added regression test TestResolve_ProjectVolumeNamesOverrideDefaults.
close_reason: Fixed in same changeset while wiring volume list

title: Implement havn build for base image
description:
Replace the build command stub with the real base-image build flow. The CLI and domain build path already exist in part; this issue makes them build the spec-defined base image using host UID/GID and the repository build context.

title: Align startup auto-build with base-image contract

Align startup auto-build with the same base-image contract used by havn build. Startup currently assumes it can build a missing image, but that path must use the same context and build arguments as the explicit build command.

notes: Startup missing-image recovery now reuses container.Build with the same base-image contract as havn build (context docker/, Dockerfile default, UID/GID build args from host), and coverage now asserts these contract fields during StartOrAttach image auto-build.

title: Write base-image implementation spec
description:
The overview spec defers all concrete base-image behavior to a missing implementation spec. Write that spec first so Dockerfile, init, and build wiring have one authoritative contract.

## Acceptance

title: Wire root start-or-attach command
description:
Replace the root command stub with the real start-or-attach flow. This command is the main product entrypoint and must resolve config, derive the project container name, ensure shared infrastructure, and exec into the dev shell.

title: Wire list command to container domain
description:
Replace the list command stub with the real container listing flow. The domain package already returns havn-managed container info; the CLI needs to invoke it and format human and JSON output.

Wired stop command to container domain via injected StopBackend. The stop command with a target now calls container.Stop. The stop command with --all now calls container.StopAll, reports per-container status, preserves best-effort semantics, emits JSON action results in json mode, and returns exit code 1 when any stop-all targets fail.

title: Expand CLI deps and Docker-backed adapters
description:
CLI wiring is blocked by dependency shape, not missing domain logic. The command tree exists, but production dependency construction is incomplete and several domain consumer interfaces do not line up directly with internal/docker.Client. Define the production-side adapters and services the CLI will invoke.

## Scope

title: Base image spec and Dockerfile
description: havn-overview.md defers to an implementation spec that does not exist. Write the spec covering: Dockerfile (Ubuntu 24.04, Nix, devuser, sshd), entrypoint/init script, UID/GID mapping, and build context structure. Then implement. Blocks havn build and full e2e container startup.
notes: Resolved decisions: base-image contract now lives in specs/base-image.md; build context is docker/ with Dockerfile relative to that context; devuser UID/GID must match the host via build-time args; runtime assumptions for tini, sleep, sudo, and /usr/sbin/sshd are explicit; remaining work is implementation of the Docker assets and aligning startup auto-build with the shared build contract.

title: Wire CLI commands to domain code
description: All 12 stub commands return ErrNotImplemented despite complete domain implementations existing. Wire Execute() deps (add DoltManager, ContainerBackend to Deps struct; initialize DoctorBackend, VolumeManager) and implement RunE functions that call domain code and format output. This is the critical path to a working CLI.
notes: Resolved decisions: current stub count is 13 RunE paths including the root action; havn list means running havn-managed project containers only; the root command propagates the attached shell exit code on success; havn dolt import must ensure shared-Dolt readiness before migration; .beads/config.yaml remains optional and is not a CLI wiring dependency.

title: Fix havn-overview.md beads env var list to match shared-dolt-server.md
description:
specs/havn-overview.md §Beads integration (lines 563-573) lists only 4 env vars (BEADS_DOLT_SHARED_SERVER, BEADS_DOLT_SERVER_HOST, BEADS_DOLT_SERVER_PORT, BEADS_DOLT_AUTO_START). specs/shared-dolt-server.md §'How beads sees it' (lines 211-218) lists the full 6 env vars including BEADS_DOLT_SERVER_USER and BEADS_DOLT_SERVER_DATABASE.

The overview must match the authoritative detail spec.

## Scope

- No code changes

## Specs
- specs/shared-dolt-server.md §'How beads sees it' (lines 211-218)
- specs/havn-overview.md §'Beads integration' (lines 563-573)

## Acceptance

close_reason: Added BEADS_DOLT_SERVER_USER and BEADS_DOLT_SERVER_DATABASE to havn-overview.md §Beads integration. All 6 env vars now match shared-dolt-server.md §How beads sees it. Committed as 572f54b.

title: gitignore 'havn' pattern matches cmd/havn/ directory, blocking git add
description: The .gitignore has a bare 'havn' pattern (line 4) intended to ignore the built binary. This also matches 'cmd/havn/', preventing 'git add cmd/havn/main.go'. Fix: change 'havn' to '/havn' to anchor it to the repo root.
notes: Fixed in commit 0f491a9 — changed .gitignore pattern from 'havn' to '/havn'
close_reason: Fixed: anchored gitignore pattern to repo root

title: Integration: quality gates pass on completed skeleton
description: Final verification that the complete skeleton passes all quality gates. Run 'make check' (fmt + lint + test + build). Fix any issues. Verify: all tests pass, no lint warnings, binary builds cleanly, 'havn --help' shows all commands, 'havn list' returns errNotImplemented, 'havn dolt start' returns errNotImplemented. This task gates the epic -- it confirms the skeleton is ready for feature work.
close_reason: All quality gates pass: fmt, build, test, lint. havn --help shows all commands, all leaf commands return errNotImplemented.

title: Scaffold output helper for stream separation and mode switching
description: Create internal/cli/output.go with an output helper that enforces stream separation. Responsibilities: status messages to stderr (all modes), data output to stdout (JSON or human-formatted), action results to stdout in JSON mode only. The helper is constructed from the global flags (--json, --verbose) and injected into commands. Exact API emerges from TDD -- start with Status(msg) and ensure stream separation is structural. Tests: status goes to stderr not stdout, data goes to stdout not stderr, JSON mode produces valid JSON on stdout, verbose mode does not affect stdout.
close_reason: Implemented output helper with TDD: Output struct with Status (stderr), Data (stdout), DataJSON (stdout, valid JSON), Verbose (stderr when enabled), IsJSON accessor. All tests pass, lint clean.

title: Implement error handling: ExitError, formatError, JSON error output
description: Create internal/cli/errors.go with: ExitError type (Code int, Err error), formatError function that translates domain error types to user-facing messages, exitCode helper that extracts ExitError code or defaults to 1. Update Execute() to use formatError and exitCode. In JSON mode (--json flag), errors are written to stderr as JSON ({"error": "message"}). In normal mode, errors are printed as 'Error: message'. Tests: ExitError with custom code, default exit code 1 for plain errors, JSON error format on stderr when --json is set, normal error format without --json.
close_reason: Implemented ExitError, ExitCode, FormatError, Output.Error with JSON/normal modes. Updated Execute() to use new error infrastructure. All tests pass.

title: Wire all subcommands with errNotImplemented stubs
description: Create all subcommands per the command tree in cli-framework.md Section 2. Each command gets its own file in internal/cli/. Parent commands (config, volume, dolt) have no RunE -- they print help. Leaf commands return errNotImplemented wrapped with the command name. Commands: list, stop (with --all and --yes local flags), build, config show, volume list, doctor (with --all local flag), dolt start/stop/status/databases/drop/connect/import/export. Tests: each command is reachable (executeCommand returns errNotImplemented, not 'unknown command').
close_reason: All subcommands wired with errNotImplemented stubs. 15 files added, all tests pass, pre-commit hooks green.

title: Define root command with persistent and local flags
description: Define the root command (havn [flags] [path]) with: persistent flags (--json, --verbose, --config), local container flags (--shell, --env, --cpus, --memory, --port, --no-dolt, --image), optional positional arg for path defaulting to '.'. Root RunE returns errNotImplemented. SilenceErrors and SilenceUsage set to true. Tests: verify flag parsing, verify --help output includes all flags, verify path arg defaults to '.'.
close_reason: Implemented root command with persistent flags (--json, --verbose, --config), local container flags (--shell, --env, --cpus, --memory, --port, --no-dolt, --image), optional [path] positional arg with MaximumNArgs(1), ErrNotImplemented sentinel, SilenceErrors/SilenceUsage. All tests pass, committed and pushed.

title: Add Cobra dependency and wire minimal main.go
description: Add spf13/cobra to go.mod. Replace the current main.go with the minimal wiring: os.Exit(cli.Execute()). Create internal/cli/root.go with NewRoot(deps Deps) and Execute() returning exit code. The root command should print 'havn' (matching current behavior) until replaced by real logic. Verify: 'go build' succeeds, 'go run ./cmd/havn' prints help.
close_reason: Cobra wired: NewRoot(Deps), Execute() -> exit code, main.go calls os.Exit(cli.Execute()). Tests pass, lint clean.

title: CLI skeleton: Cobra command tree with all commands wired
description: Build the havn CLI skeleton using Cobra per specs/cli-framework.md. All commands wired with stub implementations (errNotImplemented), global/local flags defined, output helper scaffolded, tests verifying command wiring. This is Phase 0 -- the foundation that every future feature plugs into.
notes: Completed CLI skeleton. Added missing pieces from spec: --yes flag on dolt drop, positional arg validation for dolt drop/import/export and stop, version support via ldflags. All tests pass, lint clean.
close_reason: CLI skeleton complete. All commands wired with stubs, flags/args per spec, output helper scaffolded, error handling, version support. All tests pass, lint clean.

title: Update specs and user docs for runtime path split
description:
Update support-status labels and derivative user documentation only after runtime behavior is implemented and verified.

## Scope
- Update relevant spec support statuses when the contract is actually shipped.
- Update user docs explaining host path vs container path.
- Document project mount layout under `/home/devuser/<host-home-relative-path>`.
- Document old-layout container recreation guidance.
- Remove/adjust stale beads home workaround guidance.

## Specs
- `specs/project-container-runtime.md`
- `specs/cli-framework.md`
- `specs/havn-doctor.md`

## Wires up
- Keeps specs and user-facing docs aligned with completed behavior.

## Acceptance
- Specs do not claim implemented behavior before it exists.
- README/CLI/doctor docs explain host vs container project paths.
- Docs state `havn list` shows host paths by default.
- Docs explain old-layout containers need recreation.
- Beads guidance no longer recommends obsolete `HOME=/home/<host-user>` workarounds inside project containers.

title: Split doctor host and container project path handling
description:
Update doctor so host-tier checks use host project paths and container-tier checks use container project paths.

## Scope
- Model doctor targets with both host and container project paths.
- Probe project mount and beads health from container project path.
- Keep host `.beads/` detection on host project path.
- Interpret `havn.path` as host path and `havn.path.container` as optional container path.
- Compute fallback container path for old labels when possible.
- Report actionable diagnostics when fallback computation is impossible.
- Report old-layout project mount drift diagnostically.

## Specs
- `specs/havn-doctor.md`
- `specs/project-container-runtime.md`
- `specs/environment-interface.md`

## Wires up
- Aligns doctor with runtime path split and label/mount inspection.

## Acceptance
- `project_mount` probes `/home/devuser/work/api`.
- Host `.beads/` checks use `/home/alice/work/api/.beads`.
- `doctor --all` treats `havn.path` as host project path.
- `doctor --all` uses `havn.path.container` when present.
- Missing computable container path produces an actionable diagnostic.
- `beads_health` runs with `PWD=/home/devuser/work/api` and `HOME=/home/devuser`.
- Doctor reports old-layout drift instead of treating it as healthy.

title: Label containers with host and container project paths
description:
Preserve `havn.path` as the host project path and add `havn.path.container` for the in-container project path.

## Scope

- Add `havn.path.container=<ContainerProjectPath>`.

- Treat missing `havn.path.container` as old/backward-compatible state rather than reinterpreting `havn.path`.

- `specs/cli-framework.md`

## Wires up

## Acceptance

title: Expose inspect labels and mounts in domain state
description:
Extend container inspect domain state so runtime and doctor code can reason about labels and mount layout without importing Docker SDK types.

Added adapter tests for start/doctor mapping and updated docker container info test coverage.

title: Reject incompatible project mount layouts before reuse
description:
Detect and reject old-layout project containers before start or attach instead of silently reusing incompatible mounts.

## Scope
- Inspect existing project container mount layout.
- Compare actual project bind target against expected `ContainerProjectPath`.
- Fail before interactive attach for running old-layout containers.
- Fail before start for stopped old-layout containers.
- Return structured/actionable drift errors, including JSON details where applicable.

## Specs
- `specs/project-container-runtime.md`
- `specs/cli-framework.md`
- `specs/havn-doctor.md`

## Wires up
- Protects startup and enter flows from reusing containers mounted at the host path.

## Acceptance
- Running old-layout containers are rejected before attach.
- Stopped old-layout containers are rejected before start.
- Correct-layout running containers attach successfully.
- Correct-layout stopped containers start successfully.
- Missing project mount is reported as incompatible layout.
- JSON errors include container, host project path, expected target, and actual target when known.

title: Add workdir-aware non-interactive startup execs

Expose Docker exec working directory through domain interfaces so startup validation and preparation run from the container project path without shell `cd` hacks.

## Scope

## Acceptance

- Startup prepare runs from `/home/devuser/work/api`.

title: Use container project path for havn enter

title: Improve browser-access ergonomics for containerized notebook tooling

Users running agent sessions inside havn containers can start browser-based tools (for example marimo) that are reachable in-container but not obvious to access from the host browser. Existing support via config ports works, but discoverability is low and the --port flag is SSH-only, which is easy to misinterpret.

notes: Implemented phase-local progress tracking for startup checks: classified stderr progress events are now remembered and appended to heartbeat messages as last activity context (e.g. evaluate/fetch/build). Added regression test covering heartbeat context emission during long-running validation.

title: Implement Nix startup-progress classifier from stderr lines
description:
Add a classifier that converts Nix stderr lines into stable activity categories and optional counters so users can see meaningful progress context.

## Scope

- Parse available counters/bytes/rates when present
- Produce concise status-ready events and reason classification

## Out of scope
- New CLI flags or mode controls

## Specs
- specs/cli-framework.md §Startup phase observability contract
- specs/cli-framework.md §Output modes

## Acceptance
- Given representative Nix output lines, classifier emits stable categories and extracted metrics when available
- Unknown lines degrade gracefully without noisy output
notes: Implemented startup stderr classifier for validation/prepare phases. It maps lines to evaluate/fetch/build/other and extracts optional counters (n/m), bytes, and transfer rate when present. Progress status now emits concise classified messages and preserves unknown lines gracefully.

title: Add streaming hooks for non-interactive Docker exec output
description:
Expose incremental stdout/stderr from non-interactive container exec so startup checks can observe in-flight command activity instead of only post-exit buffers.

## Scope
- Extend Docker exec boundary to support line/event streaming for non-interactive exec
- Keep current return semantics (ExecResult, exit code handling)
- Ensure startup checks can consume stderr events without changing command behavior

## Out of scope
- Changing existing ContainerExec call sites that do not opt into streaming

## Specs
- specs/cli-framework.md §Startup phase observability contract
- specs/cli-framework.md §Startup logging contract

## Wires up
- Provides the runtime output channel required for startup-check progress classification and phase-level progress updates

## Acceptance
- Startup-check exec path can receive real-time stderr events while command is running
- Existing non-streaming callers remain behavior-compatible (additive API; default behavior unchanged)
- Exit code and error semantics for existing callers are unchanged
notes: Implemented additive non-interactive exec stderr streaming hook via docker.ExecOpts.OnStderrLine and wired startup checks to consume streamed stderr lines when backend supports ContainerExecStreaming. Existing non-streaming callers keep prior behavior and exit semantics.

title: Improve havn up progress output with actionable phase/sub-step status
description:
Problem: 'havn up --prepare .' can spend many minutes in startup phases (especially validation) while output only shows elapsed-time heartbeats (e.g., 'still running (XmYs elapsed)'). This confirms liveness but not activity, making it hard to tell whether the process is healthy, where time is spent, or why it is slow after substantial Nix/dev-environment changes.

Requested behavior: During long-running startup phases (notably validation/prepare), surface concise, accurate progress context:
- current sub-step (e.g., resolving flake inputs, evaluating dev shell, fetching store paths, building derivations)
- compact progress counters when available (paths fetched, derivations built, bytes/rate)
- reason classification for slowness (network-bound fetch, CPU-bound build, evaluation-heavy)
- periodic 'last meaningful event' updates
- end-of-phase summary with useful breakdown/counts

Acceptance criteria:
1) For phases >30s, output includes sub-step context beyond elapsed timer.
2) If Nix progress metrics are available, expose them in compact form.
3) User can distinguish evaluate vs fetch vs build activity.
4) Completion line includes duration plus at least one meaningful breakdown or count.
5) Default output remains concise (no noisy raw logs).

Non-goals:
- No behavioral change to environment setup logic.
- No requirement to print verbose raw Nix logs by default.

Example (illustrative):
- validation: resolving flake inputs
- validation: flake.lock changed, full re-evaluation expected
- validation: fetching store paths 23/118 (1.2GiB/4.8GiB @ 18MiB/s)
- validation: building derivations 7/42
- validation completed in 7m7s (eval/fetch/build breakdown)
notes: Added dominant activity reason classification to startup phase completion summaries based on streamed progress activity counts (evaluate/fetch/build). Validation completion now reports likely slowness cause (evaluation-heavy, network-bound fetch, CPU-bound build). Added test coverage in internal/container/start_test.go.

title: Add contract-matrix checks to local pre-commit gate path
description:
Bring local pre-commit quality gates closer to contract-matrix intent enforced in CI.

title: Add runtime probe coverage for BASE-002 image contract
description:

## Scope

title: Improve havn doctor Dolt UX and mode clarity
description:
Problem: The human-readable output for 'havn doctor' and 'havn doctor --dolt' looks too similar, so users struggle to understand what changed between modes. Today the key differences are subtle status changes (skip/error/pass) that are easy to miss.

Requested change: Make the doctor UX easier to scan and reason about by presenting Dolt diagnostics more clearly.

Desired outcome:
- 'havn doctor' should provide a clear all-at-once diagnosis view with a dedicated Shared Dolt section.
- When project Dolt is disabled, Dolt diagnostics should be clearly marked as skipped/disabled (or hidden in a predictable way), not ambiguous.
- Reassess '--dolt' semantics so it is easier to justify for humans (e.g., focused Dolt-only view/filter) rather than a near-identical mode with subtle differences.

Acceptance ideas:
- A user can immediately tell why outputs differ between default and Dolt-focused runs without reading every line.
- Output includes explicit mode/scope messaging for Dolt diagnostics.
- Spec/docs and tests are updated to match the revised UX contract.

title: Document Dolt status observability contract and manual runtime-port verification
