notes: Updated shared-Dolt status docs to define configured_port as config-derived intent (not runtime listening fact), expanded canonical JSON examples for running/not-running states, and documented Docker-native manual runtime-port verification paths when mismatch is suspected.

title: Improve human status output with configured-port clarity
description: Update human-mode `havn dolt status` output to include clear configured-port information and concise guidance that runtime port verification is external when needed.
notes: Human-mode 'havn dolt status' now reports configured SQL port from effective config and explicitly states runtime port verification is external (docker inspect/docker port). Added CLI tests for both running and not-running output paths.

title: Add status regression coverage for configured-vs-runtime distinction
description:
Add tests asserting JSON and human output include explicit `configured_port` semantics and do not imply runtime-port verification.

Include running and not-running status cases.
notes: Added regression coverage for "havn dolt status" configured-vs-runtime semantics in both JSON and human output. JSON assertions cover configured_port for running and not-running states, and human assertions cover configured-port messaging plus explicit external runtime-verification guidance.

title: Add explicit configured_port to Dolt status payload
description:
Add `configured_port` to `havn dolt status --json` as an explicitly configuration-derived field.

Field naming and docs must make clear this is configured intent, not verified runtime listening-port state.
notes: Verified implementation already present on main: dolt status JSON includes configured_port from effective config; docs/spec explicitly state this is configured intent (not runtime listening-port). Confirmed with go test ./internal/cli -run TestDoltStatusCommand -count=1.
close_reason: Already implemented and verified on main

title: Preserve runtime-fidelity contract for Dolt status fields
description:
Define and enforce status-field fidelity rules so configured values and runtime-observed values are clearly separated.

Ensure `configured_port` is never represented as runtime port and avoid ambiguous field naming.
notes: Updated dolt status contract to use configured_sql_port (configured intent only), refreshed CLI tests for running/non-running JSON payloads, and aligned shared-dolt spec + guide wording to remove ambiguous configured_port naming.

title: Add regression coverage for ownership-boundary contracts
description:
Add tests that assert ownership-boundary behavior remains stable, including command messaging and output contracts for non-goal migration surfaces (`havn dolt import/export`).

Coverage should prevent future drift where havn re-assumes migration policy semantics.
notes: Added CLI regression coverage for ownership-boundary contracts on havn dolt import/export: JSON responses now assert stable ownership_boundary=beads_migration_workflow, import warnings are normalized to [] in JSON, and both commands emit stable boundary messaging on stderr to avoid migration-policy ownership drift.

title: Update specs and derivative docs for migration ownership handoff
description:
Update authoritative and derivative docs so shared-Dolt responsibility is infrastructure-only for havn and migration semantics are explicit non-goals owned by beads/Dolt workflows.

Ensure docs consistently direct users to beads/Dolt responsibility boundaries for migration policy.
notes: Updated specs and derivative docs to reinforce infrastructure-only shared-Dolt ownership: removed migration ownership wording from havn-overview, clarified migration semantics as beads/Dolt-owned in dolt-beads-guide, and added CLI reference ownership-boundary guidance for import/export.

title: Define command-boundary behavior for non-goal migration surfaces
description: Codify what `havn dolt import/export` may report at CLI boundary (execution/result framing) while explicitly excluding migration correctness semantics from havn ownership.
notes: Defined import/export command-boundary contract in specs/shared-dolt-server.md (execution/result framing, stable JSON fields, command-scoped failures, explicit non-goal semantics) and aligned specs/cli-framework.md ownership note. Added CLI tests for command-scoped failure framing on import/export.

title: Standardize ownership-boundary diagnostics in human and JSON output
description:
Provide stable diagnostics indicating infrastructure-owned behavior versus migration non-goals owned by beads/Dolt workflows, across human and JSON output.

Include consistent messaging for import/export surfaces so users understand where authoritative migration semantics live.
notes: Standardized ownership-boundary diagnostics for dolt import/export: introduced shared boundary code constant (beads_migration_workflow), updated human status guidance to include stable discriminator, and aligned CLI tests to assert stable human+JSON diagnostics.

title: Codify import/export as non-goals for shared-Dolt ownership
description:
Define and document that `havn dolt import/export` migration semantics are non-goals for shared-Dolt ownership.

Capture brief rationale that migration correctness belongs to beads/Dolt workflows, not havn infrastructure management.
notes: Codified import/export migration semantics as explicit shared-Dolt non-goals in specs/shared-dolt-server.md, including rationale that migration correctness policy belongs to beads/Dolt workflows while havn owns infrastructure lifecycle and command framing. Landed in 8cbb3f8.

title: Route migration workflows to beads in command guidance
description:
Update user guidance from `havn dolt` surfaces so migration-related remediation and workflow instructions point to beads-owned commands and docs.

Ensure guidance is explicit about ownership boundaries and does not imply havn owns migration correctness policy.
notes: Updated migration-facing guidance in startup notice and doctor remediation to route users to beads-owned workflow/docs ('bd migrate --help' and docs/dolt-beads-guide.md) instead of 'havn dolt import'. Added regression assertions in dolt and doctor tests.

title: Add startup progress and completion UX for image acquisition path
description: Provide clear status messaging for the auto-pull path in human mode, including when image acquisition starts, whether startup resumed, and final completion/failure context.
notes: Added human-mode startup progress messaging for Dolt image acquisition path: emits acquisition start, resume, and completion/failure context. Implemented missing-image recovery by translating image-not-found from Docker backend, pulling image, retrying container create, and covered flow with CLI and manager tests.

title: Add regression coverage for Dolt image lifecycle reliability scenarios
description:
Add coverage for key scenarios: image already present, image missing then pull succeeds, pull failure, and create/start failure after successful pull.

Include behavior expectations for both human and JSON modes where applicable.
notes: Added regression coverage for Dolt image lifecycle scenarios: image-present startup does not pull, missing-image pull success path, pull failure classification, and failures after successful pull (retry create/start). Added CLI coverage confirming human-mode acquisition progress messaging and JSON-mode suppression of acquisition-specific progress while still returning JSON success payload.
close_reason: Completed regression coverage and validated quality gates

title: Document shared-Dolt image lifecycle contract and operator expectations
description: Update specs/docs to codify auto-pull behavior, failure semantics, and user/operator expectations for fresh environments and offline/registry-constrained contexts.
notes: Updated shared-Dolt spec and derivative docs to define missing-image auto-pull contract, deterministic failure semantics, and operator expectations for fresh/offline or registry-constrained hosts (including pre-seeding guidance).

title: Implement missing-image auto-pull flow for shared Dolt startup
description:
Add startup behavior so `havn dolt start` automatically pulls the configured Dolt image when it is not present locally, then proceeds with normal container create/start/readiness flow.

Preserve deterministic failure semantics when pull or subsequent startup steps fail.
notes: Added regression coverage for missing-image auto-pull startup path: when pull succeeds but readiness times out, Start returns HealthCheckTimeoutError and records one image pull. Verified via go test ./internal/dolt ./internal/cli and pre-commit gates.

title: Classify Dolt image lifecycle failures with actionable remediation
description:
Define and implement clear failure classification and user-facing diagnostics for image lifecycle paths (image missing, pull failed, auth/registry/network errors, create/start failures after pull).

Ensure messages remain command-scoped and actionable.
notes: Implemented Dolt startup failure classification in CLI formatting: pull auth/network/image-reference failures now emit actionable remediation, plus explicit guidance for create/start failures after image acquisition. Added regression coverage in internal/cli/errors_test.go for each lifecycle class and updated generic StartError guidance to command-scoped retry/logs instructions.

title: Add doctor parity regression coverage for Dolt workflows
description:
Add regression coverage proving parity between explicit havn dolt workflows and doctor diagnostics.

Include representative scenarios such as missing Dolt image, server absent/not running, and Dolt disabled in project config while explicit Dolt diagnostics are requested.
notes: Added regression coverage for explicit doctor Dolt diagnostics: --dolt now forces shared-Dolt host checks even when config disables Dolt, derives default database from project path, and reports missing Dolt image distinctly from server-not-running.

title: Make Dolt command remediation guidance point to correct doctor usage
description: Update havn dolt remediation guidance so when doctor is recommended it points to the appropriate doctor invocation for Dolt infrastructure diagnostics, avoiding dead-end guidance when project dolt.enabled is false.
notes: Updated generic havn dolt start remediation to recommend 'havn doctor --dolt' so diagnostics remain actionable when project dolt.enabled is false; covered by cli FormatError regression test.

title: Define output and skip-reason contract for doctor Dolt mode
description: Establish stable output expectations for Dolt-mode doctor checks, including check identifiers used, skip semantics, and actionable recommendation text so behavior is consistent across human and JSON output.
notes: Implemented Dolt-mode skip contract end-to-end: prerequisite-driven skips now carry actionable recommendations (docker_daemon => Docker startup guidance; dolt_server => rerun guidance for 'havn doctor --dolt'), emitted in both human and JSON output. Added CLI regression coverage for --dolt JSON skip recommendations and updated specs/havn-doctor.md to codify --dolt surface + skip/recommendation contract.

title: Introduce explicit doctor mode for shared Dolt infrastructure diagnostics
description:
Define and add an explicit doctor mode/flag that runs shared-Dolt infrastructure checks independent of project dolt.enabled state.

Scope includes clear mode semantics, interaction with existing default and --all behavior, and command help text so users can intentionally diagnose havn dolt infrastructure when explicit Dolt commands fail.
notes: Added explicit --dolt help text clarifying it only changes shared-Dolt host diagnostics while container scope remains controlled by --all; documented the same semantics in doctor troubleshooting and added CLI regression test coverage.

title: Align doctor check-selection logic with explicit Dolt mode semantics
description:
Update doctor check-selection boundaries so shared-Dolt host/container checks execute under the explicit Dolt doctor mode even when the current project config has Dolt disabled.

Preserve existing config-scoped behavior for default doctor runs while ensuring deterministic parity in explicit Dolt diagnostic mode.
notes: Implemented explicit Dolt-mode parity for container checks: ContainerChecks now treats explicit --dolt as enabling dolt_connectivity regardless of project dolt.enabled, and doctor CLI passes explicit mode through container target planning. Added CLI regression coverage for --dolt + dolt.enabled=false to assert dolt_connectivity executes (not skip).

title: Add CLI boundary tests for Dolt command diagnostics and UX contracts
description:
Expand CLI boundary tests for havn dolt command UX and diagnostics behavior.

Include coverage for command-scoped remediation text, explicit success signaling in human mode, structured JSON error behavior, and stable handling of representative failure scenarios.
notes: Added CLI boundary-confidence coverage for Dolt drop confirmation errors (human/json) and typed JSON parse-error payloads; also asserted explicit human completion signals for start/stop/drop/import/export and implemented matching status messages.

title: Standardize Dolt command warning presentation and severity language
description:
Normalize warning output across havn dolt commands with consistent wording and severity cues.

Goal: users can distinguish warning vs failure quickly and understand whether follow-up action is required.
notes: Standardized human-mode warning severity for dolt import/export by prefixing warning-class status lines with WARNING:. Import now prefixes transfer warnings and ownership-boundary guidance consistently; JSON success payloads are unchanged.

title: Make Dolt action commands emit explicit human-mode completion signals
description:
Ensure action-oriented havn dolt commands provide clear completion messaging in human mode after successful execution, not only progress lines.

Applies primarily to lifecycle and infrastructure flows, plus import/export command surfaces where completion signaling remains user-facing.

title: Replace generic Dolt failure hints with command-specific remediation
description:
Refine havn dolt error messages so primary guidance is specific to the detected failure mode instead of generic run-doctor wording.

Scope includes common failures such as missing configured Dolt image, server not running, ownership conflicts, readiness failures, and connectivity-related errors on lifecycle and compatibility command surfaces.

Goal: users can take the next corrective action directly from the error message.
notes: Updated CLI Dolt error formatting to provide command-specific remediation for ownership conflicts, server-not-running, readiness timeouts, startup connectivity failures, and database-create connectivity failures; removed generic doctor fallback guidance in favor of actionable next steps.

title: Clarify non-goal messaging for dolt import/export surfaces
description:
Improve command-time UX so `havn dolt import/export` clearly communicate they are non-goal migration surfaces for shared-Dolt ownership and direct users to beads/Dolt workflows for migration semantics.

This task covers user-facing clarity and remediation guidance only.
notes: Updated import/export ownership-boundary warning text to include explicit migration guidance: run 'bd migrate --help' and see docs/dolt-beads-guide.md. Added CLI tests asserting this guidance appears for both import and export paths.

title: Standardize success and status messaging across action commands
description:
Make human-mode success/status messaging consistent for action-oriented commands, including explicit completion messaging where needed, while preserving existing stream-separation rules.

Scope includes harmonizing conventions used by command surfaces such as `havn up`, `havn stop`, `havn build`, and `havn dolt` lifecycle plus import/export command surfaces (with migration semantics explicitly out of scope).
notes:
Added explicit build completion status messaging:  now prints 'Base image built' on stderr after successful builds, and contract coverage now asserts this completion signal in build CLI tests.
Added explicit build completion status messaging: the build command now prints 'Base image built' on stderr after successful builds, and build CLI contract coverage now asserts this completion signal.

title: Define and ratify cross-command output contract
description:
Establish a single CLI output contract for human and JSON modes across core commands (`havn`, `up`, `enter`, `stop`, `list`, `build`, `doctor`, `config show`, `volume list`, `dolt *`).

Include:
- required success/result semantics per command type (action vs query)
- consistent stream-separation expectations (`stdout` data, `stderr` status/errors)
- stable field naming and presence rules for JSON payloads
- command-scoped error framing consistency rules

Deliverable is contract/spec alignment that downstream implementation tasks can enforce.
notes: Ratified cross-command output contract in specs/cli-framework.md with command-type semantics, command-to-type mapping for havn/up/enter/stop/list/build/doctor/config show/volume list/dolt surfaces, JSON field naming/presence rules, command-scoped JSON error framing, and explicit havn up startup_check_phases payload contract.

title: Unify JSON success payload shape across command surfaces
description:
Align JSON success/result payloads across commands to follow consistent shape rules for status/message plus command-specific fields.

Scope includes identifying and resolving cross-command drift in field naming/presence and ensuring machine consumers can rely on stable semantics.
notes: Unified action success JSON for dolt start/stop/drop to always include status+message plus command identity fields (container for start/stop, database for drop). Added CLI tests to lock contract coverage.

title: Add CLI boundary coverage for output contract invariants
description:
Add/expand CLI boundary-confidence tests that assert output contract invariants across representative command surfaces.

Include coverage for:
- stdout/stderr separation in human and JSON modes
- consistent success payload conventions
- error payload consistency in JSON mode
- command-level output behavior that should remain stable for users and automation.
notes: Expanded process-boundary CLI contract tests for query-vs-action JSON invariants: config show JSON now asserts no action wrapper fields; doctor --json asserts query payload on stdout with status/summary/checks; JSON fallback error shape now checked across action-validation and flag-parse failures.

title: Align user-facing docs with finalized output contract
description:
Update derivative docs and command references so documented output behavior matches implemented and specified CLI output contract.

Include command examples and expectations for `havn list`, action command success messages, and JSON output semantics where relevant.
notes: Updated derivative docs to align with finalized output contract: clarified stdout/stderr JSON semantics, documented action-vs-query output behavior, added stable JSON success examples, and added explicit havn list human/JSON output examples in docs/cli-reference.md and README.md.

title: havn list omits shared havn-dolt container
description:
## Summary
`havn list` does not include the shared Dolt server container (`havn-dolt`) even when it is running and managed by havn.

## Reproduction
1. Start the shared server with `havn dolt start`.
2. Confirm runtime containers with Docker:
   - `docker ps --filter "name=^havn" --format "table {{.Names}}\t{{.Image}}\t{{.Ports}}"`
3. Run `havn list`.

## Actual
- Docker shows both `havn-dolt` and project container(s).
- `havn list` only shows project container(s), omitting `havn-dolt`.

## Expected
- `havn list` includes havn-managed shared infrastructure containers such as `havn-dolt` alongside project containers.

## Notes
This affects operator visibility and makes CLI output inconsistent with Docker state for havn-managed infrastructure.
notes: Fixed by changing container list filtering to include all running managed-by=havn containers, including shared infrastructure like havn-dolt. Added regression test TestList_IncludesRunningSharedInfrastructureContainers.

title: Dolt Command UX and Actionable Diagnostics
description:
Improve havn dolt command UX so failures include command-scoped, actionable remediation guidance for common root causes (image missing, ownership conflicts, readiness failures, daemon/network issues), and make success states explicit in human output.

Scope boundary:
- This epic owns direct command-time UX and remediation guidance for havn dolt surfaces.
- For `havn dolt import/export`, this epic owns command UX only, not migration correctness or identity policy semantics.
- It does not own doctor coverage policy or doctor-check semantics; that belongs to Doctor and Command Diagnostic Parity (havn-csk).

Goal:
Reduce ambiguity and operator recovery time during direct Dolt command usage.

title: Shared-Dolt Migration Ownership Boundary and beads Handoff
description:
Narrow shared-Dolt responsibility so havn owns infrastructure only, while beads/Dolt workflows own migration correctness and project identity policy.

Scope:
- align epics and docs with infrastructure-only ownership
- define and codify `havn dolt import/export` as non-goals for shared-Dolt ownership
- ensure user guidance routes migration workflows to beads/Dolt responsibilities

Goal:
Eliminate cross-tool policy duplication and keep migration trust semantics outside havn shared-Dolt ownership.

title: Status and Observability Fidelity
description:
Improve shared-Dolt status observability so runtime state is complete enough for troubleshooting and automation, with stable JSON shape and alignment with other status-style command outputs.

Scope boundary:
- This epic owns which state facts are exposed (including explicit configured-vs-runtime distinctions).
- It does not own global output wording/stream policy; that belongs to CLI Output Consistency and Information Contract (havn-j39).

Goal:
Make status payloads operationally sufficient and trustworthy across supported paths without conflating configured values with runtime facts.

title: Shared-Dolt Image Lifecycle Reliability
description: Standardize behavior when the configured Dolt image is unavailable, including deterministic preflight or failure policy and clear operator guidance, so first-run and fresh-environment Dolt startup is predictable.

title: Doctor and Command Diagnostic Parity
description:
Align havn doctor coverage and messaging with explicit havn dolt workflows so doctor guidance is relevant when commands recommend running diagnostics.

Scope boundary:
- This epic owns doctor-versus-command diagnostic parity, coverage boundaries, and recommendation coherence.
- It does not own direct command UX copy/flow improvements; that belongs to Dolt Command UX and Actionable Diagnostics (havn-04n).

Goal:
Ensure diagnostics are coherent across direct command execution and doctor output.

title: CLI Output Consistency and Information Contract
description:
Define and enforce a shared CLI output contract across commands in human and JSON modes, including success messaging, status semantics, error framing, and stdout/stderr routing.

Scope boundary:
- This epic owns output shape, wording consistency, and stream/contract behavior.
- It does not own adding new runtime state fields; that belongs to Status and Observability Fidelity (havn-7wj).

Goal:
Ensure Dolt command output is consistent with the rest of the CLI and reliably informative for both humans and automation.

title: Add end-to-end startup-check contract tests at CLI/process boundaries
description: Expand automated coverage to verify startup-check contract behavior across unit/contract/process boundaries, including default 'up', '--validate', '--prepare', output-mode behavior, and regression matrix expectations.
acceptance_criteria:

- Process-boundary tests assert stream routing and exit semantics for startup-check flows
- Regression matrix reflects new startup-check model without legacy assumptions

title: Align startup-check CLI/help surfaces with contract
description: Ensure command registration, flag parsing, and command/help text accurately communicate the startup-check model: default lifecycle-only 'havn up', '--validate' required-check mode, and '--prepare' validate+prepare behavior. Confirm '--shell' remains rejected for up.
acceptance_criteria:
- 'havn up' exposes '--validate' and '--prepare' with contract-accurate help text

notes: Updated root/up/enter help surfaces to reflect workflow split; up now documents lifecycle-only default and startup-check modes, --prepare help explicitly states implication of --validate, and tests cover the contract language plus explicit unsupported --shell behavior on up.

title: Harden startup-check error and exit semantics in human/json modes
description: Land command-boundary conformance for startup-check failures/successes across human and JSON output modes, including command-scoped error framing, stream separation, and stable success/error payload expectations for startup-check invocations.
acceptance_criteria:
- Startup-check failures surface command-scoped actionable errors

notes: Implemented command-scoped startup-check error framing for 'havn up' (--validate/--prepare) and added stable JSON success payload fields for up outcomes: status, message, container, project_path, startup_checks. Added CLI contract tests and updated cli-framework spec command notes.

title: Update user-facing docs for startup-check workflow
description: Update user-facing documentation surfaces (README/docs and command help examples) so they explain when validation/prepare run, how to use '--validate' and '--prepare', and how default lifecycle-only 'havn up' supports debugging-first workflows.
acceptance_criteria:

- Docs explain validate and prepare intent and usage examples

- Documentation statements align with implemented CLI behavior
notes:
Updated README, CLI reference, and configuration guide to document lifecycle-only 'havn up' default plus --validate/--prepare behavior and debugging-first 'havn up' then 'havn enter' flow. Added  command help examples and coverage in internal/cli/up_test.go.
Updated README, CLI reference, and configuration guide to document lifecycle-only havn up default plus --validate/--prepare behavior and debugging-first flow (havn up then havn enter). Added havn up command help examples and coverage in internal/cli/up_test.go.

title: Introduce startup-check phase telemetry model in container startup
description: Add a startup-check phase telemetry model for validation/preparation execution in container startup orchestration. Capture phase boundaries, durations, outcomes, and interruption context in a structured way that can drive both human status output and JSON summaries.
acceptance_criteria:
- Startup-check phases have explicit start/finish/error/cancel outcomes

notes: Implemented startup-check phase telemetry model in internal/container with explicit start/finish/error/cancel outcomes, duration accounting, and interruption context (context_canceled/deadline_exceeded). Wired telemetry recording into validation and prepare orchestration via optional StartDeps.StartupCheckTelemetry and added unit tests for telemetry lifecycle plus orchestration-level accounting.

title: Add human-visible phase progress, heartbeat, and interrupt messaging
description: Surface startup-check observability on stderr for root and up startup-check flows: phase start/finish messages, elapsed timing, heartbeat updates at least every 10 seconds for active phases, and interrupted-phase reporting on cancellation.
acceptance_criteria:

Added actionable guidance for validation failures and test coverage for status/heartbeat/interrupt/failure contracts.

title: Expose phase summary in JSON output for startup-check commands

acceptance_criteria:

notes:

Implemented StartOptions telemetry passthrough and wired havn up --json to include startup_check_phases final summary entries with phase, outcome, and duration_ms from terminal phase events. Added CLI coverage for validate-mode JSON phase summary.

title: Add regression coverage for startup-check observability contracts

acceptance_criteria:

title: Refactor startup flow so default no-attach path skips validation/prepare
description: Update startup orchestration so lifecycle-only no-attach runs perform container lifecycle steps (inspect/create/start/init) without required devShell validation or optional havn-session-prepare execution. Ensure attach-oriented path behavior remains unchanged.

notes: Refactored StartOrAttachWithOptions to gate startup checks by mode: default no-attach now skips validation/prepare while attach flow keeps checks. Updated container tests for running/new/stopped no-attach defaults.

title: Add explicit startup-check controls to startup options model
description: Introduce an explicit startup-check control model in container startup options so callers can choose lifecycle-only vs validation-only vs validation+prepare behavior without relying on implicit mode coupling.
acceptance_criteria:

title: Wire havn up flags --validate and --prepare to startup behavior

title: Update startup and CLI tests for lifecycle-only up contract

acceptance_criteria:

- Tests assert '--validate' and '--prepare' run expected phases

- Contract-level CLI tests cover exit/error behavior for new flag combinations

title: Land startup-check contract end-to-end (flags, tests, docs)
description: Deliver end-to-end contract conformance for the new startup-check model across CLI flags, error/exit semantics, output contracts, and regression coverage. Ensure command/help/docs surfaces explain when validation/prepare run, and that automated tests cover default 'up' behavior plus '--validate'/'--prepare' pathways.

title: Add phase-level observability for startup checks

Ensure behavior matches spec expectations for concise default output and richer '--verbose' diagnostics.

title: Make 'havn up' lifecycle-only by default
description: Implement the spec shift so 'havn up [path]' only handles container lifecycle by default (resolve path/config, ensure infra, create/start/init, exit) and does not run environment validation or session prepare unless explicitly requested. Add/adjust command behavior for '--validate' and '--prepare' so startup checks are opt-in and maintain non-interactive semantics.

title: Align stop [name|path] contract text in specs and docs
description:
The published command contract should explicitly describe how `havn stop` interprets names versus path-like inputs so user expectations and runtime behavior remain consistent.

## Scope
- Update normative and derivative CLI docs to define path-like input handling for `havn stop [name|path]`.
- Document that relative path-like values (including `.`) are valid path targets.
- Document invalid path behavior consistently with implemented CLI semantics.

## Specs
- specs/cli-framework.md §67
- specs/cli-framework.md §285

## Wires up
- CLI reference and spec wording for stop target interpretation.

## Acceptance
- Specs/docs explicitly state how `havn stop` distinguishes container-name tokens from path-like inputs.
- Examples include relative path usage (`havn stop .`) and remain consistent with implemented behavior.
- No contradiction remains between spec text, docs text, and runtime behavior.
notes: Aligned normative and derivative contracts for havn stop target interpretation, including relative path example (havn stop .) and path-error semantics. Updated specs/cli-framework.md and docs/cli-reference.md in c8b9e61.

title: Normalize havn stop target resolution for path-like inputs
description:
`havn stop [name|path]` currently accepts the argument shape but only derives container names from absolute paths. Relative path-like targets (for example `.` and `./repo`) are treated as literal container names, causing false "not found" errors.

## Scope
- Classify stop target inputs so path-like values (`.`, `..`, values containing path separators, and absolute paths) are handled as filesystem paths.
- Resolve path-like inputs to canonical project paths and derive container names consistently with existing name-derivation rules.
- Require path-like targets to resolve to an existing directory; return actionable CLI errors when invalid.
- Preserve literal-name behavior for non-path-like tokens.
- Cover behavior with domain + CLI regression tests for representative cases (`.`, `./x`, `../x`, absolute path, literal container name, invalid/missing path).

## Specs
- specs/cli-framework.md §67
- specs/cli-framework.md §285

## Wires up
- `havn stop` target parsing and container-name resolution path in stop domain/CLI boundary.

## Acceptance
- `havn stop .` from a project directory stops that project container instead of attempting to stop container `"."`.
- Relative and absolute path-like targets resolve to the same container naming behavior as equivalent canonical paths.
- Literal container names continue to work unchanged.
- Invalid path-like targets fail with a clear path-related error, not a misleading container-name error.
notes: Implemented path-like stop target resolution: '.', '..', relative/absolute separator-containing targets resolve to canonical existing directories, then derive container names. Added domain and CLI regression tests for dot/relative targets and invalid path-like errors. Landed in c8b9e61.

title: havn stop fails for relative path targets like '.'
description:
`havn stop [name|path]` currently treats only absolute paths as paths. Relative path targets (for example `.` or `./project`) are passed through as literal container names, causing failures like `container "." not found`.

Repro:
1. Ensure a running project container exists for current repo (`havn list` shows `havn-<user>-<project>`).
2. Run `havn stop .` from the project directory.
3. Observe: `Error: havn stop: container "." not found`.

Expected:
- Relative path targets should resolve to the project container name the same way absolute paths do.
- `havn stop .` should stop the container for the current project.

Notes:
- Current resolution logic in `internal/container/stop.go` only derives container names for absolute paths.
- Existing tests cover absolute path derivation but not relative path inputs.

title: Add copy/paste starter template for havn-compatible environments
description: Provide a practical starter template in docs/environment-interface.md that environment authors can copy directly.

title: Fix nix registry preparer ownership regression across startup and enter paths
description:
Context: after epic `havn-7qo` landed, `havn enter <path>` now runs Nix registry preparation, but registry writes can still fail in interactive enter sessions.

Observed behavior:
- `havn up .`
- `havn enter .`
- `nix registry add flake:devenv "github:jorgengundersen/dev-environments?dir=environments/default"`
- error: `opening file "/home/devuser/.config/nix/registry.json": Permission denied`

Container inspection shows the symlink is correct but target ownership is wrong:
- `/home/devuser/.config/nix/registry.json -> /home/devuser/.local/state/nix/registry.json`
- `/home/devuser/.local/state/nix/registry.json` is `root:root` and mode `0644`

Why this is a bug:
- Interactive enter workflow should allow `devuser` to mutate Nix registry aliases.
- Current behavior keeps alias persistence path but leaves it unwritable for the normal container user.

Expected behavior:
- After `havn enter <path>`, `nix registry add/remove` succeeds without manual `sudo chown`.
- Prepared registry file and symlink target are writable by `devuser`.

Likely area:
- `internal/cli/nix_registry_preparer.go` write path (`CopyToContainer`) appears to produce root-owned state registry file.

Acceptance:
- Repro above succeeds without permission errors.
- Prepared `/home/devuser/.local/state/nix/registry.json` is owned by `devuser:devuser` (or otherwise writable by `devuser`).
- Add regression test coverage for ownership/permissions in registry preparer behavior.
acceptance_criteria:
- `havn enter <path>` allows `nix registry add/remove` without manual ownership repair.
- Prepared `/home/devuser/.local/state/nix/registry.json` is writable by `devuser` (owner `devuser:devuser` or equivalent write-safe permissions).
- Fix applies to all paths using `nixRegistryPreparer` (`havn`, `havn up`, `havn enter`).
- Unit tests cover ownership/permission correction behavior in registry preparer write paths.
- Existing alias-preservation behavior and malformed-state safety checks remain intact.
notes:
Implementation context for assignee:
- Repro-confirmed file state inside container after prep:
  - `/home/devuser/.config/nix/registry.json -> /home/devuser/.local/state/nix/registry.json`
  - `/home/devuser/.local/state/nix/registry.json` owned by `root:root`, mode `0644`
- This likely impacts all command paths that invoke `nixRegistryPreparer` (`havn`, `havn up`, `havn enter`), not only enter.
- Suspected cause: registry file write uses `CopyToContainer` in `internal/cli/nix_registry_preparer.go`; docker copy path in `internal/docker/copy.go` does not enforce `devuser` ownership.
- Suggested fix: after writing state registry file, enforce owner/mode for `devuser` on `/home/devuser/.local/state/nix/registry.json` (and parent dir if needed), then wire legacy symlink. Keep idempotent and avoid rewriting valid user data.
- Test guidance: extend `internal/cli/nix_registry_preparer_test.go` to assert ownership-repair command(s) are issued in write paths; retain existing no-rewrite behavior when state already valid.
Fixed nix registry prep ownership regression by enforcing  after writes and during no-rewrite prep paths before symlink wiring. Added regression test coverage in internal/cli/nix_registry_preparer_test.go for ownership repair while preserving alias-state behavior.
Implemented ownership repair by running chown devuser:devuser /home/devuser/.local/state/nix/registry.json after write and on no-rewrite prepare paths before legacy symlink wiring. Added regression test coverage in internal/cli/nix_registry_preparer_test.go for ownership correction and preserved alias-state behavior.

title: Align havn enter with Nix registry persistence contract
description:
Context: `havn enter [path]` currently inspects/attaches only and does not run Nix registry preparation, while startup paths (`havn [path]` / `havn up [path]`) do. This causes user-facing failures when running `nix registry add ...` in enter sessions because Nix defaults to writing `~/.config/nix/registry.json` and that path may be unwritable in existing containers unless havn has wired the registry symlink/state path.

Spec/documentation mismatch: `specs/configuration.md` (Nix Registry Persistence contract) states that during startup AND interactive entry, havn configures in-container Nix to use `/home/devuser/.local/state/nix/registry.json`. Current implementation satisfies startup but not enter.

Observed behavior:
- `havn enter .` -> `nix registry add ...` can fail with permission denied opening `/home/devuser/.config/nix/registry.json`
- `havn .` path typically avoids this by running registry preparation

Epic goal: make interactive entry (`havn enter`) consistent with startup so Nix registry alias operations work predictably and persist via `volumes.state` without requiring users to run full startup first.

Expected outcomes:
1) `havn enter` executes equivalent registry preparation before attach
2) behavior aligns with spec + docs for interactive entry
3) regression tests cover success and prep-failure paths for enter
4) docs/notes updated to remove ambiguity about enter vs startup alias behavior

Non-goals:
- changing host-global Nix config
- introducing image-level persistence hacks
- requiring users to pass `--registry` manually for normal enter workflows

title: Persist Nix flake registry aliases via havn-state without global config side effects

Scope includes design and implementation of Nix-scoped configuration behavior (for example using NIX_CONFIG or user nix.conf wiring), migration and safety considerations, and documentation so users can adopt stable short flake aliases safely.

title: Implement startup behavior for sticky container resources
description:
Implement startup behavior that preserves resource limits for existing containers and applies effective config only on new container creation.

## Scope
- Ensure running/stopped existing project containers are reused without mutating CPU/memory/memory_swap limits.
- Ensure newly created project containers use resolved effective config values (including defaults when no custom overrides are set).
- Add/adjust tests that cover reuse versus recreate behavior.

## Specs
- specs/configuration.md
- specs/havn-overview.md

## Wires up
- internal/container startup orchestration and effective-config handoff to container creation.

## Acceptance
- Reusing an existing container does not change previously applied resource limits.
- Recreating a container applies current effective limits, including defaults when no custom resource overrides are provided.
notes: Added startup contract coverage: existing stopped containers are reused without applying new resource values, and newly created containers from root startup apply effective default resources (4 CPUs, 8g memory, 12g memory+swap).

title: Define startup resource semantics and defaults in specs

## Scope

- Specify recreate behavior: when a new project container is created without custom overrides, defaults are applied (cpus=4, memory=8g, memory_swap=12g).

title: Set Docker resource defaults and Nix memory safety baseline

Scope includes defining defaults, making them configurable, validating codex builds under constrained resources, and documenting operational/debug guidance. Do not decompose into child issues yet.

title: Fix default Nix setup to persistently enable nix-command and flakes
description:
Running `nix flake show` in a default havn environment fails with:

`experimental Nix feature "nix-command" is disabled; use "--extra-experimental-features nix-command" to override`

This indicates default setup does not persistently enable required Nix experimental features for flake-based workflows. Users should not need per-command overrides for standard havn/Nix usage.

## Scope
- Ensure default environment configuration enables `nix-command` and `flakes` persistently (not only via per-command flags).
- Apply the fix in the correct default config layer used by havn environments.
- Update user-facing docs/specs where behavior/requirements are defined.

## Specs
- specs/havn-overview.md
- specs/configuration.md
- specs/base-image.md
- specs/code-standards.md
- specs/test-standards.md

## Wires up
- Default havn environment bootstrap to flake-capable Nix CLI behavior without manual flags.

## Acceptance
- In a default havn environment, `nix flake show` runs without requiring `--extra-experimental-features` flags.
- Behavior is persistent across shells/sessions in that environment.
- Tests cover the configured default behavior at the relevant boundary.
- Docs/specs are updated to reflect the default-enabled behavior and any constraints.
notes: Implemented persistent flake support in the base image by writing /etc/nix/nix.conf with 'experimental-features = nix-command flakes'; added a Dockerfile contract test and updated specs/base-image.md.

- Confirmation appears for both newly-started and already-running container paths.
- Failure paths remain error-first with non-zero exit and no false success message.
notes: Added explicit success confirmation in havn up after StartOrAttach returns: stderr now prints container + project identity (Container <name> is running for project <path>) and a new CLI test locks this behavior.

description:
Implement a focused follow-up for havn-rqx:
