title: dolt: automatic migration detection at startup
description:
When `havn .` starts a project container, detect existing local beads databases that could be migrated to the shared server and inform the user. This is a non-blocking informational message — startup continues normally.

## Scope
- During container startup (after dolt.enabled check, before or after ensure-ready), check for:
  1. `dolt.enabled: true` in project config
  2. An existing `.beads/dolt/<dbname>/` directory with a `.dolt/` subfolder in the project
  3. The database does NOT exist on the shared server
- When all three conditions are met, print an informational message:
  ```
  Existing beads database found at .beads/dolt/<dbname>/
  Not yet on shared server. Run `havn dolt import .` to migrate.
  ```
- Startup is NOT blocked. No interactive prompt.

## Specs
- specs/shared-dolt-server.md §'Automatic migration detection' (lines 554-567)

## Wires up
- Integrated into container startup path (havn-x0d.4) at step 7 (dolt setup)
- Requires dolt.EnsureReady (havn-a8n.2) to check if database exists on server

## Acceptance
- Detection triggers only when all 3 conditions are met
- Message is informational, not an error or warning
- Startup continues normally regardless of detection result
- No detection when dolt.enabled is false
- No detection when .beads/dolt/ does not exist
close_reason: Implemented DetectMigration on Setup with MigrationHint return type. 5 tests cover: all conditions met, no local DB, DB already on server, error propagation, correct path construction. Commit 053c4c7.

title: doctor: tier-2 container checks
description:
Add 7 tier-2 container-level checks that run inside running havn containers via one-shot exec. Supports --all flag to check every running container (default: current directory container only).

## Scope
- 7 tier-2 checks (in spec order), all prereq docker_daemon:
  1. nix_store — test -d /nix/store via exec. Error if missing.
  2. nix_devshell — nix develop <ref>#<shell> --command true via exec. 60s timeout (override from default 10s). Warning on failure.
  3. project_mount — test -w <project_path> via exec. Error if missing/unwritable.
  4. config_mounts — test -r / test -w for each config mount entry. Warning per missing mount.
  5. ssh_agent — test -S $SSH_AUTH_SOCK + ssh-add -l via exec. Warning on failure.
  6. dolt_connectivity — NetworkInspect to verify container is on havn-net. Conditional: Dolt enabled. Error if not connected.
  7. beads_health — which bd + bd doctor --json via exec. Conditional: .beads/ exists. Surfaces beads warnings/errors.
- Container targeting: default is current directory container (derive name from cwd). --all iterates every running managed-by=havn container. Tier-2 skipped entirely if no relevant container is running (informational message).
- Each check scoped to a specific container; results tagged with container name in output.

## Specs
- specs/havn-doctor.md §'Tier 2: Container-level checks' (lines 116-189): all 7 checks
- specs/havn-doctor.md §'Check Identifiers' (lines 322-328): tier-2 identifiers
- specs/havn-doctor.md §'Behavior Notes' (lines 330-353): timeouts, partial runs

## Wires up
- havn doctor --all runs tier-2 checks across all running containers.
- havn doctor (no --all) runs tier-2 for current directory container only.

## Acceptance
- All 7 tier-2 checks run with correct stable identifiers.
- nix_devshell uses 60s timeout (not default 10s).
- --all runs tier-2 for every running havn container; results tagged with container name.
- No relevant container running causes tier-2 skipped with informational message (not an error).
- Conditional checks (dolt_connectivity, beads_health) skipped when not applicable.
- Check results appear in all three output modes (human, verbose, JSON) with container name.
close_reason: All 7 tier-2 container checks implemented with TDD, framework extended, CLI wired with --all flag

title: doctor: tier-1 host checks + framework + runner + formatters
description:
Create `internal/doctor/` with the check framework (interface, runner, skip logic, timeouts, output formatters, exit codes) and all 8 tier-1 host-level checks. Bundled as one task because the framework has no observable value without checks, and the checks need the framework to run.

## Scope
- `internal/doctor/check.go`: Check interface with stable ID, tier, prerequisite IDs, and Run(ctx) CheckResult. CheckResult{Status, Message, Detail, Recommendation} where Status is pass/warn/error/skip.
- `internal/doctor/runner.go`: Runner that executes checks in registered order, skips checks whose prerequisites failed (status: "skip" with reason), enforces per-check timeout (default 10s, configurable per check), and collects all results into Report{Status, Summary{Passed, Warnings, Errors}, Checks []CheckResult}.
- `internal/doctor/format.go`: three output formatters:
  - Human (default): [pass]/[warn]/[error] per check, recommendations indented.
  - Verbose (--verbose): adds detail line per check.
  - JSON (--json): structured object matching havn-doctor.md §"JSON output".
- Exit code mapping: all pass → 0, any warn → 1, any error → 2. Via ExitError.
- `internal/doctor/backend.go`: DoctorBackend interface — Ping, Info, ImageInspect, NetworkInspect, VolumeInspect, ContainerInspect, ContainerExec. Consumer-defined.
- 8 tier-1 checks (in spec order):
  1. docker_daemon — Ping/Info. No prerequisite.
  2. base_image — ImageInspect for configured image. Prereq: docker_daemon.
  3. network — NetworkInspect for configured network. Prereq: docker_daemon.
  4. volumes — check each expected volume exists (uses config volume names + Dolt volumes if enabled). Prereq: docker_daemon.
  5. global_config — config.LoadFile on ~/.config/havn/config.toml. No prereq.
  6. project_config — config.LoadFile + Validate on .havn/config.toml. No prereq.
  7. dolt_server — ContainerInspect (running + managed-by=havn label) + SELECT 1 via exec. Prereq: docker_daemon. Conditional: skipped if Dolt not enabled.
  8. dolt_database — SHOW DATABASES via exec, check project db exists. Prereq: dolt_server. Conditional: skipped if not in project context.
- First-run friendliness: informational note when multiple "auto-created on first start" warnings (missing volumes/network).
- Wire DoctorRunner into cli.Deps.

## Specs
- specs/havn-doctor.md (entire spec): check definitions, output formats, JSON schema, exit codes, behavior notes
- specs/havn-overview.md §Diagnostics (lines 595-627): CLI interface

## Wires up
- havn doctor CLI command calls Runner, formats output, returns exit code.
- Reuses config.LoadFile/Validate for checks 5/6.

## Acceptance
- All 8 tier-1 checks run in spec order with correct stable identifiers.
- Failed prerequisite causes dependent checks to report status: "skip".
- Docker daemon down causes checks 2/3/4/7/8 skipped; checks 5/6 still run.
- Per-check timeout of 10s enforced (timeout produces error status).
- Human output matches havn-doctor.md §"Human-readable" format.
- Verbose output includes detail lines per havn-doctor.md §"Verbose" format.
- JSON output matches havn-doctor.md §"JSON output" schema exactly.
- Exit code: 0 all pass, 1 any warn, 2 any error.
close_reason: Implemented: internal/doctor/ with Check interface, Runner (prerequisite skip, timeouts), 3 formatters (human/verbose/JSON), all 8 tier-1 host checks, Backend interface, wired into CLI. All tests and lints pass.

title: dolt: import + export via docker cp
description:
Migrate Dolt databases between project directories and the shared server using file copy operations.

## Scope
- `internal/dolt/migrate.go`:
  - `Import(ctx, projectPath string, cfg config.Config) error` — read db name from config (or derive from dir name), locate .beads/dolt/<db>/ in project, check if db already exists on server (error if exists without --force flag), tar + CopyToContainer to /var/lib/dolt/<db>/, verify with SHOW DATABASES, verify project_id match between .beads/metadata.json and database metadata table (warn on mismatch).
  - `Export(ctx, dbName, destPath string) error` — CopyFromContainer from /var/lib/dolt/<db>/ to <destPath>/.beads/dolt/<db>/, untar.
- Uses DoltBackend CopyToContainer/CopyFromContainer + ContainerExec (for verification).

## Specs
- specs/shared-dolt-server.md §'Migration strategies / Strategy 1' (lines 460-483): import flow
- specs/shared-dolt-server.md §'The export command' (lines 539-550): export flow
- specs/shared-dolt-server.md §'Beads project_id validation' (lines 419-448): project_id verification

## Wires up
- havn dolt import <path> CLI command calls dolt.Import.
- havn dolt export <name> CLI command calls dolt.Export.

## Acceptance
- Import copies a local .beads/dolt/<db>/ directory into the havn-dolt-data volume.
- Import errors if the database already exists on the server (without --force).
- Import verifies the database is visible via SHOW DATABASES after copy.
- Import warns (does not error) on project_id mismatch.
- Export copies a database from the volume to the specified destination path.
close_reason: Implemented Import and Export on Manager with full TDD. Import: derives db name, checks source dir, checks server existence (with --force override), tars and copies via CopyToContainer, verifies with SHOW DATABASES, warns on project_id mismatch. Export: checks server existence, copies via CopyFromContainer, untars to dest. Added CopyFromContainer to Backend interface, 7 new tests, all lint clean.

title: dolt: server lifecycle (start, stop, status)
description:
Create `internal/dolt/` with the shared Dolt server container lifecycle. Handles starting the havn-dolt container (with config.yaml generation and health-check polling), stopping it, and querying its status. Defines the package backend interface and error types.

## Scope
- `internal/dolt/backend.go`: `DoltBackend` interface — ContainerCreate, ContainerStart, ContainerStop, ContainerInspect, ContainerExec (one-shot), CopyToContainer. docker.Client satisfies this.
- `internal/dolt/manager.go`: `Manager` struct with injected DoltBackend.
  - `Start(ctx, cfg config.Config) error` — if havn-dolt exists: verify managed-by=havn label (NotManagedError if missing), start if stopped. If not exists: generate config.yaml, copy to havn-dolt-config volume via CopyToContainer, create container per shared-dolt-server.md §"Container creation" (image, network, restart policy, env, volumes, labels). Poll health check (SELECT 1 via one-shot exec) with timeout.
  - `Stop(ctx) error` — stop havn-dolt container.
  - `Status(ctx) (DoltStatus, error)` — inspect container, return DoltStatus matching havn dolt status --json shape (running, container, image, port, network, managed_by_havn). Not running returns {Running: false}.
- `internal/dolt/config.go`: `GenerateConfig(cfg config.Config) []byte` — pure function producing the Dolt server config.yaml (listener host/port, timeouts, data_dir, autocommit).
- `internal/dolt/errors.go`: StartError, HealthCheckTimeoutError, NotManagedError — all implementing TypedError.
- Extend FormatError in internal/cli/errors.go for all three error types per havn-overview.md error table.

## Specs
- specs/shared-dolt-server.md §'Container creation' (lines 54-74): docker run args
- specs/shared-dolt-server.md §'Server configuration' (lines 78-98): config.yaml shape
- specs/shared-dolt-server.md §'Lifecycle Management' (lines 128-151): ownership check, health poll
- specs/havn-overview.md §'havn dolt status --json' (lines 222-240): output shape
- specs/havn-overview.md error table rows 7a, 7b

## Wires up
- havn dolt start/stop/status CLI commands.
- Foundation for all other dolt tasks.

## Acceptance
- Start creates havn-dolt container with correct args when it does not exist.
- Start with existing havn-dolt missing managed-by=havn label returns *NotManagedError.
- Start polls health check until SELECT 1 succeeds; returns *HealthCheckTimeoutError on timeout.
- Stop stops the havn-dolt container.
- Status returns populated DoltStatus when running; {Running: false} when not.
- GenerateConfig produces valid YAML matching the spec shape.
- All error types implement TypedError.
close_reason: Implemented internal/dolt/ with Backend interface, Manager (Start/Stop/Status), GenerateConfig, error types, and FormatError translations. All tests pass, lint clean.

title: container: start + attach (startup orchestration)
description:
The core of havn — implement steps 3-10 of the startup sequence. Check if a container is already running (attach if so), ensure infrastructure (image, network, volumes), delegate Dolt setup, create the container with full mounts/env/labels, run post-start init, and attach to the devShell.

## Scope
- `internal/container/start.go`:
  - `StartOrAttach(ctx, deps StartDeps, cfg config.Config, projectPath string) (int, error)` — returns the exit code from the attached shell session.
  - `StartDeps` aggregates all injected dependencies: ContainerBackend, ImageBackend, NetworkBackend, VolumeEnsurer, MountResolver, DoltSetup (optional), ExecBackend (one-shot + interactive tty).
- Steps implemented:
  - 3: Inspect container by derived name. If running, interactive tty exec (attach to existing).
  - 4: ImageExists then Build if missing (reuses n3 logic or calls ImageBackend directly).
  - 5: NetworkInspect then NetworkCreate if missing.
  - 6: Delegate to VolumeEnsurer for each expected volume.
  - 7: If cfg.Dolt.Enabled, delegate to DoltSetup interface (provided by dolt domain). Receives beads env vars back.
  - 8: Build CreateOpts — image, name, network, mounts (from MountResolver), env (SSH + beads + config.Environment), labels (managed-by=havn + havn metadata), restart policy, tty, entrypoint (tini -- sleep infinity), user.
  - 9: One-shot exec to start sshd (best-effort).
  - 10: Interactive tty exec with nix develop <ref>#<shell> -c bash and --workdir <projectPath>.
- Self-healing status messages via Output (stderr): "Network havn-net not found, creating...", etc.
- On failure: abort, do not roll back completed steps.

## Out of scope
- Dolt domain logic (havn-a8n) — accessed via DoltSetup interface only.
- Dockerfile contents — build logic in n3.

## Specs
- specs/havn-overview.md §'Container Lifecycle' (lines 356-407): full startup sequence + error contracts
- specs/havn-overview.md §'Entrypoint and Init' (lines 584-593): tini + sleep infinity + sshd
- specs/shared-dolt-server.md §'When havn starts a project container' (lines 128-151): Dolt delegation + beads env vars
- specs/architecture-principles.md §5: fail fast, no rollback

## Wires up
- havn [path] CLI command calls container.StartOrAttach.
- Composes name, config, mount, volume, and docker wrapper packages into one orchestrated flow.

## Acceptance
- Running container: StartOrAttach attaches to existing container via interactive tty exec; returns shell exit code.
- New container: creates with correct mounts (from MountResolver), env vars (SSH + beads + config), labels (managed-by=havn + metadata), network, and entrypoint.
- Missing image/network/volumes are self-healed with status messages to stderr.
- Dolt enabled: DoltSetup is called; beads env vars included in container env.
- Dolt disabled: DoltSetup is skipped; no beads env vars.
- Post-start init runs sshd via one-shot exec (failure is logged, not fatal).
- User lands in nix develop <ref>#<shell> -c bash inside the container at the project path.
- Startup failure aborts with actionable error message; completed steps are not rolled back.
close_reason: Implemented StartOrAttach with full startup orchestration (steps 3-10). 16 tests covering: running container attach, new container creation with correct opts/labels/env/entrypoint, image build on miss, network creation on miss, volume ensure, Dolt setup with env var injection, best-effort sshd init, shell exit code propagation, and error abort cases.

title: container: build base image
description:
Orchestrate `havn build` — detect host UID/GID, locate the Dockerfile context, and build the base image via the Docker wrapper.

## Scope
- `internal/container/build.go`:
  - `Build(ctx, backend ImageBackend, opts BuildOpts) error`.
  - `BuildOpts`: image name (from config), Dockerfile context path, UID/GID for devuser.
  - `ImageBackend` interface: `ImageBuild(ctx, opts) error`, `ImageExists(ctx, name) (bool, error)`.
- Host UID/GID detection: os.Getuid() / os.Getgid(), passed as build args.
- Dockerfile context path: convention docker/ in the havn repo (or configurable).

## Specs
- specs/havn-overview.md §'Base Image' (lines 433-455): build behavior, UID/GID mapping
- specs/havn-overview.md CLI table: havn build

## Wires up
- havn build CLI command calls container.Build.
- Startup step 4 uses ImageBackend.ImageExists + Build to self-heal.

## Acceptance
- Build calls ImageBuild with the correct context, tag, and UID/GID build args.
- Host UID/GID are detected automatically (no user configuration needed).
- Build failure returns a wrapped error with the Docker build output.
close_reason: Implemented Build(ctx, backend, opts) with ImageBackend interface, BuildOpts (image name, context path, UID/GID), BuildError wrapping, and full test coverage via TDD. Commit c2f7be8.

title: container: stop single + stop-all best-effort
description:
Stop a specific container by name or path, or stop all havn project containers with the best-effort multi-unit pattern. Excludes havn-dolt from stop-all.

## Scope
- `internal/container/stop.go`:
  - `Stop(ctx, backend ContainerBackend, target string) error` — stop a single container by name. If target is a path, derive the container name first (via name.SplitProjectPath + name.DeriveContainerName). Returns *ContainerNotFoundError if not found.
  - `StopAll(ctx, backend ContainerBackend) (StopResult, error)` — list all managed-by=havn containers (excluding havn-dolt), stop each. Best-effort: failure on one does not abort others. Collects per-container outcome.
- `StopResult{Stopped []string, Failed []StopFailure}` where `StopFailure{Name string, Err error}`.
- Extend ContainerBackend interface with `ContainerStop(ctx, name, timeout) error`.

## Specs
- specs/havn-overview.md §Shutdown (lines 409-431): single stop, stop-all, output format, Dolt exclusion
- specs/architecture-principles.md §6: best-effort multi-unit operations

## Wires up
- havn stop <name|path> calls container.Stop.
- havn stop --all calls container.StopAll.

## Acceptance
- Stop with a valid running container stops it and returns nil.
- Stop with a missing container returns *ContainerNotFoundError.
- Stop with a path argument derives the container name and stops it.
- StopAll stops all managed-by=havn containers except havn-dolt.
- StopAll continues after individual failures and returns the full StopResult.
- StopResult includes both stopped names and failed names with errors.
close_reason: Implemented Stop and StopAll in internal/container/stop.go with full test coverage. Stop resolves name or path targets. StopAll uses best-effort semantics excluding havn-dolt. StopBackend extends Backend with ContainerStop. All acceptance criteria verified.

title: container: list running containers
description:
Create `internal/container/` with the query operation for `havn list`. Defines the package's `ContainerBackend` interface and `ContainerInfo` type that other container tasks reuse.

## Scope
- `internal/container/types.go`: `ContainerInfo` struct matching `havn list --json` shape — name (name.ContainerName), path, image, status, shell, cpus, memory, dolt (boolean). These fields are decoded from container labels set at creation time.
- `internal/container/backend.go`: `ContainerBackend` interface — the subset of Docker wrapper operations this package needs. Starts with `ContainerList(ctx, filters) ([]ContainerInfo, error)` and `ContainerInspect(ctx, name) (ContainerInfo, error)`. docker.Client satisfies this.
- `internal/container/list.go`: `List(ctx, backend ContainerBackend) ([]ContainerInfo, error)` — queries containers with `managed-by=havn` label, decodes havn metadata labels into ContainerInfo fields, returns the list.
- Label convention: `managed-by=havn`, `havn.path=<abs-path>`, `havn.shell=<shell>`, `havn.cpus=<n>`, `havn.memory=<size>`, `havn.dolt=<true|false>`. Documented in code.

## Specs
- specs/havn-overview.md §'havn list --json' (lines 138-155): output shape
- specs/architecture-principles.md §4: consumer-defined interface
- specs/code-standards.md §4: wrapper structure, compile-time assertions

## Wires up
- havn list CLI command calls container.List and formats output.
- ContainerBackend and ContainerInfo reused by stop and start tasks.

## Acceptance
- List returns []ContainerInfo matching the havn list --json shape.
- Only containers with managed-by=havn label are returned.
- ContainerInfo fields populated from labels: name, path, image, status, shell, cpus, memory, dolt.
- Empty result returns [] (not nil).
close_reason: Implemented container.List with Backend interface and Info type. Labels decoded from managed-by=havn containers. Empty result returns [] not nil. All tests and lints pass.

title: volume: list + ensure-exists with config-driven registry
description:
Create `internal/volume/` with the domain logic for listing expected havn volumes (with existence checks) and ensuring volumes exist during container startup. The volume registry is config-driven — volume names come from the merged Config, container mount paths are fixed constants.

## Scope
- `internal/volume/types.go`: `VolumeEntry{Name, Mount, Exists}` matching the `havn volume list --json` shape per havn-overview.md (name, mount path, exists boolean).
- `internal/volume/backend.go`: `VolumeBackend` interface defining what this package needs from the Docker wrapper — `VolumeInspect(ctx, name) error` (exists check) and `VolumeCreate(ctx, name) error`. docker.Client satisfies this (havn-r8z.5) but this package does not import internal/docker/.
- `internal/volume/manager.go`: `Manager` struct with injected VolumeBackend.
  - `ExpectedVolumes(cfg config.Config) []VolumeEntry` — pure function returning the volume registry: 4 configurable volumes (nix to /nix, data to /home/devuser/.local/share, cache to /home/devuser/.cache, state to /home/devuser/.local/state) plus 2 Dolt volumes (havn-dolt-data to /var/lib/dolt, havn-dolt-config to /etc/dolt/servercfg.d) when cfg.Dolt.Enabled is true.
  - `List(ctx, cfg config.Config) ([]VolumeEntry, error)` — calls ExpectedVolumes, then checks existence for each via VolumeBackend. Returns the full list with Exists populated.
  - `EnsureExists(ctx, name string) error` — creates a volume if it does not exist (inspect + create if not found). Idempotent.
- Wire volume.Manager into cli.Deps.

## Specs
- specs/havn-overview.md §'havn volume list --json' (lines 157-172): output shape
- specs/havn-overview.md §'Named volumes' (lines 459-472): volume to mount mapping
- specs/havn-overview.md §'Container Lifecycle' step 6: ensure volumes exist
- specs/havn-doctor.md §1.4 (lines 67-78): volume existence checks

## Wires up
- havn volume list command calls Manager.List and formats output.
- Container startup step 6 calls Manager.EnsureExists for each expected volume.
- Doctor §1.4 reuses Manager.List to check volume health.

## Acceptance
- ExpectedVolumes with Dolt disabled returns 4 entries; with Dolt enabled returns 6.
- List returns all entries with Exists: true when all volumes exist in Docker.
- List returns Exists: false for volumes missing from Docker (not an error — expected on first run).
- EnsureExists with an existing volume is a no-op (idempotent).
- EnsureExists with a missing volume creates it via VolumeBackend.
- Output shape matches havn volume list --json spec: [{name, mount, exists}].
close_reason: Implemented volume package: Entry type, Backend interface, Manager with ExpectedVolumes, List, and EnsureExists. Wired into cli.Deps. All tests passing.

title: mount: resolve all mount specs from config + project path
description:
Create `internal/mount/` with the pure-function logic to produce a complete list of mount specs and env vars for container creation. Takes a merged Config and a project path, returns every bind mount, named volume mount, and SSH forwarding mount the container needs — plus any env vars (SSH_AUTH_SOCK) to set inside the container.

## Scope
- `internal/mount/types.go`: `MountSpec{Source, Target, ReadOnly, Type}` where Type is "bind" or "volume". `ResolveResult{Mounts []MountSpec, Env map[string]string}`.
- `internal/mount/resolve.go`: `Resolve(cfg config.Config, projectPath, homeDir string, opts ResolveOpts) (ResolveResult, error)`.
- `ResolveOpts` bundles injectable I/O callbacks: `Glob func(pattern string) ([]string, error)`, `Exists func(path string) bool`, `SSHAuthSock string`.
- Four mount categories resolved:
  1. Project directory — always present, rw. Host path = container path.
  2. Config file mounts (mounts.config) — parse "path:mode" entries, expand ~ to homeDir, expand env vars, glob wildcards (.gitconfig-*), skip entries where host file does not exist, mount to same relative path under /home/devuser/.
  3. SSH agent — if mounts.ssh.forward_agent and SSHAuthSock is non-empty and exists: mount socket to /ssh-agent (ro), add SSH_AUTH_SOCK=/ssh-agent to env. If mounts.ssh.authorized_keys and ~/.ssh/authorized_keys exists: mount it (ro).
  4. Named volumes — from config.Volumes.* to fixed container paths (/nix, /home/devuser/.local/share, /home/devuser/.cache, /home/devuser/.local/state). Always present.
- Domain errors for invalid mount specs (malformed path:mode entries).

## Out of scope
- Dolt volume mounts (havn-dolt-data, havn-dolt-config) — handled by dolt domain.
- Port mappings — separate concern, not mounts.
- Actually executing the mounts (Docker wrapper's job).

## Specs
- specs/havn-overview.md §'Bind mounts' (lines 474-494): project dir, config mounts, SSH, wildcards
- specs/havn-overview.md §Configuration mounts section (lines 283-299): mount config shape, path relativity
- specs/havn-overview.md §'Named volumes' (lines 459-472): volume to container path mapping
- specs/havn-doctor.md §2.3 (lines 141-150): bind mount expectations
- specs/havn-doctor.md §2.4 (lines 152-159): SSH agent expectations
- specs/architecture-principles.md §1: pure function, one nameable job

## Wires up
- Container package passes ResolveResult.Mounts into docker.CreateOpts mounts field.
- Container package passes ResolveResult.Env into docker.CreateOpts env field.

## Acceptance
- Project directory mount is always present as {Source: projectPath, Target: projectPath, ReadOnly: false, Type: "bind"}.
- Config mount ".gitconfig:ro" with homeDir /home/devuser resolves to {Source: "/home/devuser/.gitconfig", Target: "/home/devuser/.gitconfig", ReadOnly: true, Type: "bind"} when the file exists; omitted when it does not.
- Wildcard ".gitconfig-*:ro" expands to one MountSpec per matching file.
- SSH agent forwarding with valid socket produces a bind mount + SSH_AUTH_SOCK=/ssh-agent in env.
- SSH agent forwarding with empty/missing socket produces no mount and no env var.
- Named volumes produce 4 MountSpecs with Type "volume".
- Malformed mount entries (missing mode, unknown mode) return a typed error.
- Deterministic output given the same inputs (config + path + injected callbacks).
close_reason: Implemented internal/mount/ with Resolve function, types, errors, and 11 tests covering all acceptance criteria

title: config: validation + error types
description:
Validate a merged Config and produce actionable error messages for invalid values. Includes ValidationError domain type with structured JSON support.

## Scope
- `Validate(cfg Config) error` — returns *ValidationError for the first invalid field, or nil.
- `internal/config/errors.go`: `ValidationError{Field, Reason}` implementing error and TypedError with type "config_validation_error" and details {"field": "...", "reason": "..."}.
- Extend FormatError in internal/cli/errors.go to unwrap *config.ParseError and *config.ValidationError to the messages from havn-overview.md error table rows 1a/1b.
- Validation rules:
  - Resources.CPUs > 0
  - Resources.Memory matches size format (digits + unit suffix like "g", "m")
  - Resources.MemorySwap valid format
  - Dolt.Port in valid port range (1-65535)
  - Ports entries match Docker port mapping format (host:container or host:container/proto)
  - Mounts.Config entries match path:mode where mode is ro or rw

## Specs
- specs/havn-overview.md error table rows 1a, 1b
- specs/code-standards.md §2 (error type pattern)
- specs/architecture-principles.md §5 (fail fast)

## Wires up
- Called after Resolve() in every CLI command path.
- Doctor checks §1.5/§1.6 use LoadFile + Validate to report config health.

## Acceptance
- Validate with valid Config returns nil.
- Validate with CPUs=0 returns *ValidationError{Field: "resources.cpus", Reason: "must be greater than 0"}.
- Validate with invalid memory format returns *ValidationError with descriptive reason.
- FormatError(*ParseError{File: "config.toml", Line: 5, Detail: "..."}) returns "Config parse error at config.toml:5: ...".
- FormatError(*ValidationError{Field: "resources.cpus", Reason: "..."}) returns "Invalid config: resources.cpus: ...".
- Both error types implement TypedError for structured JSON output.
close_reason: Implemented Validate(cfg Config) error, ValidationError + ParseError TypedError methods, and FormatError extensions for config error types. All validation rules from spec (CPUs, memory format, memory_swap, dolt.port, ports mapping, mounts.config) covered with table-driven tests.

title: config: merge with precedence + source tracking + flake resolution
description:
Implement the 5-level precedence merge (flag > env > project > global > default) with per-field source tracking, the Overrides pattern for flag/env overlay, environment variable resolution, and the special flake resolution priority chain.

## Scope
- `Overrides` struct with pointer fields (*string, *int, *bool) for each overridable config value. Nil = not set, non-nil = explicitly set. Used by CLI layer via Cobra Changed() check per cli-framework.md §4.
- `EnvOverrides() Overrides` — reads HAVN_SHELL, HAVN_ENV, HAVN_CPUS, HAVN_MEMORY, HAVN_SSH_PORT, HAVN_IMAGE from the environment and returns an Overrides with non-nil fields for those that are set.
- `Source` type — per-field map tracking where each effective value came from ("default", "global", "project", "env", "flag"). Shape matches `havn config show --json` source object.
- `Resolve(global, project Config, envOverrides, flagOverrides Overrides) (Config, Source)` — applies precedence chain and returns merged config + source map.
- Mount merging: project mounts.config entries append to global entries (not replace).
- `ResolveFlake(cfg Config, flakeExists bool) string` — pure function implementing the 5-level flake resolution priority. Caller supplies the .havn/flake.nix existence check result.

## Specs
- specs/havn-overview.md line 95: precedence chain
- specs/havn-overview.md §'havn config show --json' (lines 174-220): source tracking shape
- specs/havn-overview.md §'Dev environment flake resolution' (lines 340-349): flake priority
- specs/havn-overview.md §'Global flags' (lines 85-91): env var mappings
- specs/cli-framework.md §4 (lines 217-250): Overrides pattern, Changed() check

## Wires up
- CLI commands call Resolve() after loading files to get the effective config.
- `havn config show --json` outputs the merged Config + Source.
- Root command uses ResolveFlake() to determine the Nix flake ref.

## Acceptance
- Flag overrides take precedence over env vars, which take precedence over project config, which take precedence over global config, which take precedence over defaults.
- Nil Overrides fields do not override lower-precedence values (zero-value vs not-set is distinguished).
- Source map correctly attributes each field to its winning precedence level.
- EnvOverrides() reads the 6 documented env vars and ignores unset ones.
- ResolveFlake(cfg, true) with no flag/env/project env override returns "path:./.havn" (flake.nix exists fallback).
- Project mount entries append to global mount entries.
close_reason: Implemented Resolve with 5-level precedence (flag>env>project>global>default), Source tracking, Overrides with pointer fields, EnvOverrides for 6 HAVN_* vars, ResolveFlake with 5-level flake priority, mount config appending. All tests pass, commit dbc5023.

title: config: types + defaults + TOML loading
description:
Create `internal/config/` with the Config struct hierarchy, all default values per spec, and TOML file loading. This is the foundation task — every other config task and every downstream domain package depends on these types.

## Scope
- `internal/config/config.go`: `Config` struct with sub-structs `ResourceConfig`, `VolumeConfig`, `MountConfig`, `SSHConfig`, `DoltConfig` — all with `toml` tags matching the schema in `havn-overview.md` §Configuration. Project-only fields (`Ports []string`, `Environment map[string]string`) included in the same struct.
- `Default() Config` function returning all spec-defined default values (image `havn-base:latest`, network `havn-net`, cpus 4, memory `8g`, memory_swap `12g`, volumes havn-nix/havn-data/havn-cache/havn-state, dolt port 3308, dolt image `dolthub/dolt-sql-server:latest`, dolt enabled false).
- `LoadFile(path string) (Config, error)` — parse a TOML file into Config using BurntSushi/toml. Missing file returns zero Config + nil error (not an error per doctor §1.5). Parse error returns `*ParseError`.
- `internal/config/errors.go`: `ParseError{File, Line, Detail}` implementing `error` and `TypedError` (from havn-xx2.1) with type `config_parse_error`.
- Promote BurntSushi/toml from indirect to direct dependency in go.mod.

## Specs
- specs/havn-overview.md §Configuration (lines 255-338): full TOML schema
- specs/code-standards.md §3 (lines 159-178): Config struct pattern
- specs/havn-overview.md error table row 1a: ParseError message
- specs/havn-doctor.md §1.5, §1.6: missing file is warning, parse error is error

## Wires up
- Config types used by every downstream domain package.
- LoadFile used by Execute() to load global and project configs.

## Acceptance
- Default() returns a Config with all values matching the spec defaults.
- LoadFile with a valid TOML file returns a populated Config.
- LoadFile with a missing file returns zero Config and nil error.
- LoadFile with a malformed TOML file returns *ParseError with file path and line number.
- All struct fields round-trip through TOML (marshal then unmarshal produces equal result).
close_reason: Implemented: Config struct hierarchy, Default(), LoadFile(), ParseError. All 5 tests pass, linter clean. TypedError methods deferred to havn-xx2.1.

title: name: derive container name + path splitting + named types
description:
Create `internal/name/` with the named resource types and the pure-function logic to derive a deterministic container name from a filesystem path. This is a load-bearing primitive used by the root command, container package, and doctor checks — every project container's identity flows through it.

## Scope
- `internal/name/types.go`: `ContainerName`, `ImageName`, `NetworkName`, `VolumeName` as distinct `string` types per `code-standards.md` §3.
- `internal/name/derive.go`: `DeriveContainerName(parent, project string) ContainerName`.
  - Format: `havn-<parent>-<project>`.
  - Sanitization: lowercase, non-alphanumeric chars replaced with hyphens, consecutive hyphens collapsed, leading/trailing hyphens stripped.
  - Result is always a valid Docker container name (`[a-zA-Z0-9][a-zA-Z0-9_.-]*`).
  - Returns an error (or the derivation function returns `(ContainerName, error)`) if the sanitized name exceeds Docker practical length limit or is empty after sanitization.
- `internal/name/path.go`: `SplitProjectPath(absPath string) (parent, project string, err error)`.
  - Extracts the last two path segments from an absolute path.
  - Validation: path must be absolute; must have at least 2 segments below root.
  - Pure function — does not check filesystem existence.

## Out of scope
- Dolt database name derivation (belongs to dolt domain).
- Path existence checks (caller's responsibility).
- `ImageName`/`NetworkName`/`VolumeName` derivation — their values come from config, not from paths.

## Specs
- specs/havn-overview.md §'Primary command' (lines 50-52): derivation rule + example
- specs/code-standards.md §3 (lines 150-157): named types
- specs/code-standards.md §7 (line 377): function signature
- specs/test-standards.md (lines 192-219): table-driven test example with sanitization case
- specs/architecture-principles.md §1 (lines 79-82): domain-first naming

## Wires up
- Root command uses `SplitProjectPath` + `DeriveContainerName` at startup step 2.
- `havn stop <path>` uses the same to resolve a path argument to a container name.
- `ContainerName`, `ImageName`, `NetworkName`, `VolumeName` types used across `internal/docker/`, `internal/container/`, `internal/config/`, `internal/doctor/` as those packages land.

## Acceptance
- `DeriveContainerName("user", "api")` returns `ContainerName("havn-user-api")`.
- `DeriveContainerName("user", "my.project")` returns `ContainerName("havn-user-my-project")` (dots sanitized).
- Derivation is deterministic and idempotent.
- `SplitProjectPath("/home/devuser/Repos/github.com/user/api")` returns `("user", "api", nil)`.
- `SplitProjectPath("relative/path")` returns an error.
- `SplitProjectPath("/too-shallow")` returns an error (fewer than 2 segments below root).
- All derived names are valid Docker container names.
close_reason: Implemented internal/name/ package with named types (ContainerName, ImageName, NetworkName, VolumeName), DeriveContainerName with sanitization/validation, and SplitProjectPath with absolute-path/depth checks. All 11 tests pass, linter clean.

title: Retrofit havn-r8z error types with structured error interface
description:
After the wrapper error types exist (havn-r8z.6 is the last to define one) and the `TypedError` convention exists (havn-xx2.1), add `ErrorType()` and `ErrorDetails()` methods to all 5 wrapper error types so they emit structured JSON.

## Scope
- Add `ErrorType() string` and `ErrorDetails() map[string]any` to:
  - `DaemonUnreachableError` → type `"daemon_unreachable"`, details: empty or `{}`
  - `ImageNotFoundError` → type `"image_not_found"`, details: `{"name": "<image>"}`
  - `ContainerNotFoundError` → type `"container_not_found"`, details: `{"name": "<container>"}`
  - `NetworkNotFoundError` → type `"network_not_found"`, details: `{"name": "<network>"}`
  - `VolumeNotFoundError` → type `"volume_not_found"`, details: `{"name": "<volume>"}`
- Verify each error flows through `Output.Error` and produces the expected structured JSON.

## Specs
- specs/cli-framework.md §6
- specs/code-standards.md §2

## Acceptance
- All 5 wrapper error types implement `TypedError`.
- `Output.Error` with each produces the documented JSON shape including correct `type` and `details`.
- Type identifiers are snake_case and stable (documented in code comments).
close_reason: All 5 docker error types implement TypedError. NetworkNotFoundError and VolumeNotFoundError added with ErrorType()/ErrorDetails() methods, FormatError cases in cli/errors.go, and full test coverage including Output.Error JSON pipeline tests.

title: Structured JSON error convention + Output.Error update
description:
Define the interface convention for domain errors to expose machine-readable type identifiers and structured details for JSON output. Update `Output.Error` to emit the richer shape. Update specs to document the convention. This lands before havn-r8z.1 so the first wrapper error types adopt the convention from day one.

## Scope
- Define an interface (e.g. `TypedError`) in `internal/cli/errors.go` that domain errors can implement:
  - `ErrorType() string` — stable snake_case identifier (e.g. `daemon_unreachable`, `container_not_found`)
  - `ErrorDetails() map[string]any` — structured fields from the error (e.g. `{"name": "havn-user-api"}`)
- Update `Output.Error` to detect `TypedError` via interface assertion:
  - If implemented: emit `{"error": "<message>", "type": "<type>", "details": {<details>}}`
  - If not implemented (fallback): emit `{"error": "<message>"}` (current behavior, backwards compatible)
- Normal (non-JSON) mode is unchanged — still emits `Error: <message>` to stderr.
- Update `specs/cli-framework.md` §6: document the structured JSON error shape, field semantics (`error` always present, `type` and `details` present when the error implements the interface), stability contract (type identifiers are stable, details fields can grow but not shrink).
- Update `specs/code-standards.md` §2: add the `TypedError` interface convention alongside existing error-type guidance. Show how a domain error type implements it.

## Out of scope
- Defining specific error types or their type identifiers — each domain epic's job.
- Changing `FormatError` — havn-r8z.1 owns the first cases.

## Specs
- specs/cli-framework.md §6 (update)
- specs/code-standards.md §2 (update)
- specs/architecture-principles.md §5

## Wires up
- `Output.Error` emits richer JSON. All future domain error types that implement `TypedError` get structured JSON output for free.
- havn-r8z.1 can adopt the convention when it defines `DaemonUnreachableError`.

## Acceptance
- `Output.Error` with a `TypedError`-implementing error emits `{"error": "...", "type": "...", "details": {...}}` to stderr in JSON mode.
- `Output.Error` with a plain `error` (no `TypedError`) emits `{"error": "..."}` (fallback, no regression).
- Normal mode output is unchanged.
- `cli-framework.md` §6 documents the full JSON error shape with examples.
- `code-standards.md` §2 documents the `TypedError` interface with an implementation example.
close_reason: Implemented structured JSON error output for TypedError in Output.Error. Updated cli-framework.md §6 and code-standards.md §2 with convention docs. Commit fe2afaf.

title: docker: file copy (in/out)
description:
`docker cp` equivalent for Dolt database migration (`havn dolt import` / `havn dolt export`) and Dolt server config-file injection.

## Scope
- `internal/docker/copy.go`: `CopyToContainer(ctx, ContainerName, dstPath string, tarStream io.Reader) error`, `CopyFromContainer(ctx, ContainerName, srcPath string) (io.ReadCloser, error)`.
- Callers construct / consume tar streams using stdlib `archive/tar`.
- Error translation: missing container returns `*ContainerNotFoundError` (from havn-r8z.6). Missing source path inside container returns a wrapped error (no dedicated type for now — no caller distinguishes yet).

## Specs
- specs/shared-dolt-server.md §'Migration strategies / Strategy 1 (Direct copy)' (`docker cp <project>/.beads/dolt/<db>/ havn-dolt:/var/lib/dolt/<db>/`)
- specs/shared-dolt-server.md §'Operational Commands' (`havn dolt import / export`)
- specs/havn-overview.md CLI table (`havn dolt import`, `havn dolt export`)

## Acceptance
- `CopyToContainer` writes a tar stream to the given destination path inside the container.
- `CopyFromContainer` returns a readable tar stream of the container's source path contents; caller closes it.
- Missing container returns `*ContainerNotFoundError`.
- Context cancellation interrupts in-flight copies.
close_reason: Implemented CopyToContainer and CopyFromContainer in internal/docker/copy.go with error translation (ContainerNotFoundError) and context cancellation support. Commit 92105fe.

title: docker: container exec (interactive tty)
description:
Interactive tty attach for `havn [path]` (exec into the nix devShell) and `havn dolt connect` (Dolt SQL shell). Load-bearing for the main havn UX. Technically fiddly — raw-mode stdin, SIGWINCH resize forwarding, exit-code capture.

## Scope
- `internal/docker/exec.go`: `ContainerAttach(ctx, ContainerName, AttachOpts) (exitCode int, err error)`.
- `AttachOpts`: command (argv), env, workdir, user, and host `io.Reader` / `io.Writer` for stdin / stdout / stderr (typically `os.Stdin`, `os.Stdout`, `os.Stderr`).
- Implementation: `ContainerExecCreate` with tty=true and all three streams attached → `ContainerExecAttach` returning a `HijackedResponse` → raw-mode stdin proxy (put host terminal into raw mode, restore on exit) → `SIGWINCH` handler calling `ContainerExecResize` with new dimensions → block until container process exits → `ContainerExecInspect` for exit code.
- Missing container returns `*ContainerNotFoundError`.

## Out of scope
- Choosing the command to run (`nix develop <ref>#<shell> -c bash`, `dolt sql`) — caller supplies.
- Terminal detection ('is stdin a tty?') — caller's responsibility; wrapper assumes tty when this method is called.

## Specs
- specs/havn-overview.md §'Container Lifecycle' step 10 (`docker exec -it --workdir <path> ... nix develop ... -c bash`)
- specs/shared-dolt-server.md §'Operational Commands' (`havn dolt connect`)

## Acceptance
- Interactive shell proxies stdin/stdout/stderr with no line-buffering artifacts (readline works, ctrl-C reaches the container, etc.).
- Host terminal resize (SIGWINCH) propagates to the container's pty.
- Host terminal raw mode is restored on exit, including panic and ctx-cancel paths.
- Method returns the remote process's exit code.
- Missing container returns `*ContainerNotFoundError`.
close_reason: Implemented ContainerAttach in internal/docker/exec.go with: AttachOpts type (cmd, env, workdir, user, stdin/stdout/stderr), interactive tty exec via ContainerExecCreate+Attach, raw-mode stdin with defer restore, SIGWINCH→ContainerExecResize forwarding, exit code capture via ContainerExecInspect, ContainerNotFoundError for missing containers. Commit 2e2ac48.

title: docker: container exec (one-shot)
description:
Non-interactive container exec for doctor tier-2 checks and Dolt SQL operations. Captures stdout/stderr bytes and an exit code.

## Scope
- `internal/docker/exec.go`: `ContainerExec(ctx, ContainerName, ExecOpts) (ExecResult, error)`.
- `ExecOpts`: command (argv), env, workdir, user.
- `ExecResult`: exit code, captured stdout bytes, captured stderr bytes.
- Implementation: `ContainerExecCreate` (tty=false, attach stdout+stderr) → `ContainerExecAttach` → read streams → `ContainerExecInspect` for exit code.
- Error translation: missing container returns `*ContainerNotFoundError` (from havn-r8z.6).

## Specs
- specs/havn-doctor.md §2.1 (`test -d /nix/store`), §2.3 (`test -w`), §2.4 (`test -S`), §2.6 (`which bd`, `bd doctor --json`)
- specs/havn-doctor.md §1.7 (Dolt `SELECT 1`), §1.8 (`SHOW DATABASES`)
- specs/shared-dolt-server.md §'Lifecycle Management' (CREATE DATABASE IF NOT EXISTS)

## Acceptance
- `ContainerExec` returns `ExecResult` with exit code and captured stdout/stderr bytes for any exited command.
- A non-zero exit code does NOT return an error — the exit code is part of `ExecResult`. Only exec-plumbing failures return errors.
- Context cancellation interrupts the exec.
- Missing container returns `*ContainerNotFoundError`.
close_reason: Implemented ContainerExec with ExecOpts/ExecResult types, stdcopy demux, ContainerNotFoundError translation. Commit 35c0ce7.

title: docker: container query (list, inspect)
description:
Read-only container operations. Used by `havn list`, startup step 3 ('is container running?'), and doctor §2.5.

## Scope
- `internal/docker/container.go`: `ContainerList(ctx, ContainerListFilters) ([]ContainerInfo, error)`, `ContainerInspect(ctx, ContainerName) (ContainerInfo, error)`.
- `ContainerInfo`: ID, name, image, status (running / stopped / etc.), raw labels map, mounts (source/target/mode), network memberships, env vars. Exposes raw labels — havn-specific fields (path, shell, cpus, memory, dolt) are derived by the consumer from labels, not by the wrapper.
- `ContainerListFilters`: label filter, name-prefix filter, status filter.
- Error translation: missing container returns `*ContainerNotFoundError` (defined in havn-r8z.6).

## Specs
- specs/havn-overview.md §'havn list --json', §'Container Lifecycle' step 3
- specs/havn-doctor.md §2.5

## Acceptance
- `ContainerList` with label filter `managed-by=havn` returns only havn-owned containers; empty slice when none.
- `ContainerInspect` returns `*ContainerNotFoundError` for missing; populated `ContainerInfo` otherwise.
- `ContainerInfo.Labels` is populated verbatim from Docker (caller derives havn-specific metadata).
- `ContainerInfo.Mounts` and `ContainerInfo.Networks` are sufficient for doctor §2.5 network-membership check.
close_reason: Implemented ContainerList and ContainerInspect with ContainerInfo, MountInfo, ContainerListFilters types. All acceptance criteria met: label/name/status filtering, ContainerNotFoundError translation, Labels/Mounts/Networks populated from Docker SDK.

title: docker: container lifecycle (create, start, stop, remove)
description:
The main wrapper task — container mutation operations. Largest child of havn-r8z. Used by the root command startup path, `havn stop`, `havn stop --all`, and Dolt lifecycle.

## Scope
- `internal/docker/container.go`: `ContainerCreate(ctx, CreateOpts) (ContainerID, error)`, `ContainerStart(ctx, ContainerName) error`, `ContainerStop(ctx, ContainerName, StopOpts) error`, `ContainerRemove(ctx, ContainerName, RemoveOpts) error`.
- `CreateOpts` covers the full surface havn needs: image, name, network, bind mounts, named volume mounts, env, labels (`managed-by=havn` and other havn metadata), restart policy, tty, workdir, cmd, entrypoint, user (for UID/GID mapping).
- `StopOpts` includes graceful timeout.
- `RemoveOpts` covers force + volume cleanup.
- `internal/docker/errors.go`: add `ContainerNotFoundError`.
- `cli.FormatError` unwrap.
- Error translation: image-not-found during `ContainerCreate` returns `*ImageNotFoundError` (from havn-r8z.2). Container-not-found during start/stop/remove returns `*ContainerNotFoundError`.

## Specs
- specs/havn-overview.md §'Container Lifecycle' steps 7-8, §'Shutdown', §'Container Lifecycle' error table
- specs/shared-dolt-server.md §'Container creation'
- specs/code-standards.md §4 (wrapper structure example)
- specs/architecture-principles.md §4, §5

## Acceptance
- `ContainerCreate` accepts `CreateOpts` covering all fields used by the startup sequence (havn-overview.md steps 7-8) and the Dolt container spec (shared-dolt-server.md §'Container creation').
- Image-not-found during create returns `*ImageNotFoundError`.
- `ContainerStart` / `ContainerStop` / `ContainerRemove` return `*ContainerNotFoundError` when the target is missing.
- `ContainerStop` respects the timeout in `StopOpts`.
- Created containers carry the `managed-by=havn` label when `CreateOpts.Labels` includes it.
close_reason: Implemented ContainerCreate, ContainerStart, ContainerStop, ContainerRemove with full CreateOpts surface, StopOpts timeout, RemoveOpts force/volume cleanup. Added ContainerNotFoundError and ImageNotFoundError with TypedError support. cli.FormatError handles both. All tests pass, linter clean.

title: docker: volume ops (inspect, create, list)
description:
Volume operations for `havn` startup step 6, `havn volume list`, and doctor §1.4.

## Scope
- `internal/docker/volume.go`: `VolumeInspect(ctx, VolumeName) (VolumeInfo, error)`, `VolumeCreate(ctx, VolumeCreateOpts) error`, `VolumeList(ctx, VolumeListFilters) ([]VolumeInfo, error)`.
- `VolumeInfo` covers the fields `havn volume list --json` requires per havn-overview.md §'havn volume list --json' (name, existence; mount path comes from config, not the wrapper).
- `VolumeListFilters`: label filter (for `managed-by=havn`) and name-prefix filter.
- `internal/docker/errors.go`: add `VolumeNotFoundError`.
- `cli.FormatError` unwrap.

## Specs
- specs/havn-overview.md §'Volume list', §'Named volumes', §'havn volume list --json'
- specs/havn-doctor.md §1.4

## Acceptance
- `VolumeInspect` returns `*VolumeNotFoundError` for missing; populated `VolumeInfo` otherwise.
- `VolumeList` with `managed-by=havn` label filter returns only havn-owned volumes.
- `VolumeList` returns an empty slice (not nil) when nothing matches.
close_reason: Implemented VolumeInspect, VolumeCreate, VolumeList in internal/docker/volume.go with full test coverage. VolumeNotFoundError and FormatError unwrap were already in place from havn-xx2.

title: docker: network ops (inspect, create, list)
description:
Network operations for `havn` startup step 5, doctor §1.3, and doctor §2.5 (container-on-network check).

## Scope
- `internal/docker/network.go`: `NetworkInspect(ctx, NetworkName) (NetworkInfo, error)`, `NetworkCreate(ctx, NetworkCreateOpts) error`, `NetworkList(ctx, NetworkListFilters) ([]NetworkInfo, error)`.
- `NetworkInfo` includes name, ID, driver, and the list of connected container names/IDs (required by doctor §2.5).
- `internal/docker/errors.go`: add `NetworkNotFoundError`.
- `cli.FormatError` unwrap.
- Error translation at the SDK boundary.

## Specs
- specs/havn-overview.md §'Container Lifecycle' step 5, §'Docker Network'
- specs/havn-doctor.md §1.3, §2.5

## Acceptance
- `NetworkInspect` returns `*NetworkNotFoundError` for a missing network; populated `NetworkInfo` otherwise.
- `NetworkInfo.ConnectedContainers` is populated from the SDK response.
- `NetworkCreate` either succeeds or returns a wrapped error; the 'already exists' condition is surfaced as a distinct, detectable outcome (sentinel or typed error — pick the minimal thing and document it in the method comment).
- `NetworkList` supports filtering by name.
close_reason: Implemented NetworkInspect, NetworkCreate, NetworkList in internal/docker/network.go with full TDD. NetworkInspect returns *NetworkNotFoundError for missing networks and populates ConnectedContainers. NetworkCreate surfaces already-exists as ErrNetworkAlreadyExists sentinel. NetworkList supports name-prefix filtering. All pre-commit hooks pass (fmt, build, test, lint).

title: docker: image build
description:
Add `ImageBuild` to the Docker wrapper for `havn build`. Separate from image lookup (havn-r8z.2) because the streaming build path has its own plumbing weight (tar-packing a build context, streaming build output to the caller).

## Scope
- `internal/docker/image.go`: `ImageBuild(ctx, BuildOpts) error`.
- `BuildOpts` havn type: build-context source (directory path or `io.Reader` of a tar stream), tag, Dockerfile path relative to context, `io.Writer` for streaming build output.
- Tar-pack the context directory (stdlib `archive/tar`) when caller supplies a path rather than a pre-packed reader.
- Stream the Docker build API response to the caller's writer; surface non-zero build exit via a wrapped error.

## Specs
- specs/havn-overview.md CLI table row `havn build`
- specs/havn-overview.md §'Base Image'

## Acceptance
- `ImageBuild` with a directory context successfully builds an image and tags it.
- Build output streams live to the provided `io.Writer` (not buffered until completion).
- A failed build returns a wrapped error; the writer still receives all output up to the failure point.
- Context cancellation interrupts an in-flight build.
close_reason: Implemented ImageBuild in internal/docker/image.go with BuildOpts, tar-packing via stdlib archive/tar, streaming JSON output parsing, and ImageBuildError. All tests and linter pass.

title: docker: image ops (inspect, exists)
description:
Add image lookup operations to the Docker wrapper. Used by `havn` startup step 4 (ensure base image exists) and doctor §1.2.

## Scope
- `internal/docker/image.go`: `ImageInspect(ctx, ImageName) (ImageInfo, error)`, `ImageExists(ctx, ImageName) (bool, error)`.
- `ImageInfo` havn type in `internal/docker/types.go` (id, tag, created-at).
- `internal/docker/errors.go`: add `ImageNotFoundError`.
- Extend `cli.FormatError` to unwrap `*ImageNotFoundError` with a message guiding the user to `havn build`.
- Translate Docker SDK errors at the boundary (`errdefs.IsNotFound` → `*ImageNotFoundError`; other errors wrapped with context).

## Specs
- specs/havn-overview.md §'Container Lifecycle' step 4
- specs/havn-doctor.md §1.2
- specs/code-standards.md §2 (example)

## Acceptance
- `ImageInspect` returns `*ImageNotFoundError` for a missing image; populated `ImageInfo` otherwise.
- `ImageExists` returns `(false, nil)` for missing (not an error) and `(true, nil)` for present.
- `FormatError(&ImageNotFoundError{Name: 'havn-base:latest'})` produces an actionable message pointing to `havn build`.
close_reason: Implemented ImageInspect and ImageExists in internal/docker/image.go with full TDD coverage. ImageNotFoundError and FormatError handling already existed.

title: docker: daemon client + error translation foundation
description:
Introduce the Moby Docker SDK, create `internal/docker/` with the `Client` wrapper struct, land the first wrapper methods (`Ping`, `Info`), establish the wrapper-boundary error-translation pattern via `DaemonUnreachableError`, extend `cli.FormatError` to unwrap it, and wire a `docker.Client` into `cli.Deps`. This is the end-to-end vertical slice every subsequent wrapper task copies.

## Scope
- Add `github.com/docker/docker/client` (and required `api/types/*` subpackages) to `go.mod` on latest stable.
- `internal/docker/client.go`: `type Client struct { docker *client.Client }` (SDK client is a private field, never exposed). `NewClient(...)` constructor.
- `internal/docker/daemon.go`: `Ping(ctx) error`; `Info(ctx) (DaemonInfo, error)` returning a havn `DaemonInfo` type (server version, API version).
- `internal/docker/errors.go`: `DaemonUnreachableError` type with structured fields.
- `internal/cli/errors.go`: `FormatError` unwraps `*docker.DaemonUnreachableError` to the actionable message from the startup error table (havn-overview.md, row 'Docker daemon not running').
- `internal/cli/root.go`: add `Docker *docker.Client` field to `Deps`; `Execute()` constructs one via `NewClient`.

## Specs
- specs/architecture-principles.md §4, §5
- specs/code-standards.md §2, §4
- specs/havn-overview.md §'Container Lifecycle' error-contract table
- specs/havn-doctor.md §1.1

## Wires up
- `cli.Deps.Docker` is populated. No CLI command yet consumes it — downstream wrapper tasks and consumer epics do.

## Acceptance
- `Client.Ping` returns `*DaemonUnreachableError` when the daemon is unreachable; nil when reachable.
- `Client.Info` returns populated `DaemonInfo` when reachable; `*DaemonUnreachableError` otherwise.
- `FormatError(&DaemonUnreachableError{})` returns the actionable message ('Docker is not running. Start Docker and try again').
- `cli.Deps.Docker` is populated with a `*docker.Client` in `Execute()`. Daemon unreachability surfaces only when a method is called (the SDK client construction itself is lazy).
- No package outside `internal/docker/` imports `github.com/docker/docker/*`.
close_reason: Implemented: Docker SDK wrapper (internal/docker/) with Client, Ping, Info, DaemonUnreachableError, FormatError translation, and Deps wiring. All tests and linters pass.

title: Verify sloglint is enabled in golangci-lint config
description:
`go-simpler.org/sloglint` is already in `go.mod:199` as an indirect dep, but its actual activation in `golangci-lint` config has not been verified. Without it, future slog usage can drift from the project rules in `code-standards.md` §5 (lowercase messages, structured fields not interpolated strings, never log-and-return, no `slog.Default()` globals).

## Scope
- Locate the golangci-lint config (likely `.golangci.yml` or similar at repo root).
- Confirm `sloglint` is in the enabled linters list. Enable it if missing.
- Configure rules that match `code-standards.md` §5:
  - `no-global` / reject `slog.Default()` usage
  - `attr-only` / reject positional key-value pairs (force structured attrs)
  - message-style rules if supported
- Run `golangci-lint run` once to confirm it executes without error on the current (slog-free) codebase.

## Specs
- specs/code-standards.md §5 (logging rules)
- specs/code-standards.md §6 (linter requirement)

## Acceptance
- `sloglint` runs as part of `golangci-lint run`.
- Linter rules enforce the structured-fields and no-globals rules from `code-standards.md` §5.
- `golangci-lint run` passes on the current codebase.
close_reason: Enabled sloglint in .golangci.yml with attr-only, no-global=all, static-msg, no-mixed-args, and key-naming-case=snake. Verified it runs cleanly on current codebase. Commit 6af81f6.

title: Document slog attribute vocabulary in code-standards §5
description:
The havn-0nf epic body lists `component`, `operation`, `container_name`, `error` as standard structured attributes, but `specs/code-standards.md` §5 only shows a single `slog.String("container", name)` example. Pin the project-wide vocabulary in the spec so future domain packages don't drift into ad-hoc attribute names.

## Scope
- Append a short "Standard attributes" subsection to `specs/code-standards.md` §5 listing the attribute names, their expected value types, and which package layer typically sets each.
- Minimum set to document:
  - `component` — package name (e.g. `"docker"`, `"container"`, `"dolt"`)
  - `operation` — verb (e.g. `"start"`, `"stop"`, `"create"`)
  - `container_name` — havn-managed container name
  - `volume_name` — havn-managed volume name
  - `error` — use `slog.Any("error", err)` when logging at a handling boundary

## Specs
- specs/code-standards.md §5

## Acceptance
- `specs/code-standards.md` §5 documents the standard attribute vocabulary in a dedicated subsection.
- Future PRs that introduce slog calls have a single source of truth to reference.
close_reason: Documented standard slog attribute vocabulary in specs/code-standards.md §5. Added table with component, operation, container_name, volume_name, network_name, image, and error attributes including types, descriptions, typical layers, and usage rules.

title: Reconcile Output.Verbose helper with slog.Debug
description:
The existing `Output.Verbose(msg)` helper at `internal/cli/output.go:49–53` writes to stderr only when verbose mode is set — overlapping with what `slog.Debug` does once havn-0nf.1 lands. Decide which mechanism owns "verbose stderr diagnostics" and execute the decision before any command grows code that uses both.

Two viable options:
- **Delete `Output.Verbose`** and have callers use `logger.Debug(...)` directly. Matches `code-standards.md` §5, which never mentions a non-slog verbose path. Cleanest. Requires updating `output_test.go`.
- **Keep `Output.Verbose` as a thin wrapper** around `logger.Debug` for callers that already hold an `Output`. Matches `cli-framework.md` §5's "output abstraction" intent that the helper owns all stderr writes. Requires `Output` to hold a `*slog.Logger`.

No production code currently calls `Output.Verbose` (commands don't hold an `Output` instance), so this is a cheap decision to make now.

**Recommendation: delete `Output.Verbose`.** Fewer abstractions, one mechanism for verbose diagnostics, easier to apply the spec rule "log only at the handling boundary". Execute this by default unless a concrete reason emerges during implementation to prefer the wrapper option.

## Scope
- Delete `Output.Verbose` from `internal/cli/output.go`.
- Remove the corresponding test(s) from `internal/cli/output_test.go`.
- If during implementation the wrapper option turns out to be clearly better, document the reason in the PR and apply that instead.

## Specs
- specs/code-standards.md §5
- specs/cli-framework.md §5 (output abstraction)

## Acceptance
- Exactly one mechanism for "verbose stderr diagnostics" exists in the codebase.
- `output_test.go` reflects the chosen mechanism.
- `go test ./...` passes.
close_reason: Deleted Output.Verbose method and its tests. slog.Debug is now the single mechanism for verbose stderr diagnostics. The verbose field remains on Output for doctor format selection (different concern).

title: Construct and inject slog logger from --verbose/--json flags
description:
Wire up the project's first slog-based logger so that running `havn --verbose` produces DEBUG-level structured logs and running `havn --json` produces a JSON handler — both to stderr — and the resulting `*slog.Logger` is reachable through `cli.Deps`. This is the foundational piece of havn-0nf: once it lands, future domain packages can take a logger via constructor injection without inventing their own scheme.

Today `cli.Deps` is empty (`internal/cli/root.go:35`), `--verbose` is parsed but never read (`root.go:73`, dead-`false` at `root.go:26`), and there are no slog calls anywhere in `internal/` or `cmd/`.

## Scope
- Add `Logger *slog.Logger` to `cli.Deps`. A nil logger must be tolerated by `NewRoot` (substitute a discard handler) so existing tests calling `cli.NewRoot(cli.Deps{})` keep working.
- Implement `setupLogger(verbose, jsonOutput bool) *slog.Logger` exactly per `specs/code-standards.md` §5 lines 273–287: text handler by default, JSON handler when `jsonOutput`, both to `os.Stderr`, level INFO, DEBUG when `verbose`.
- Decide between `cmd/havn/` and `internal/cli/` as the home for `setupLogger`. Spec is mildly contradictory: `code-standards.md:270` says "at program start in `cmd/havn/`" while `cli-framework.md:117–133` insists `cmd/havn/main.go` is minimal. Pick one and document the choice in the PR.
- Wire flag values into the logger via `PersistentPreRunE` on the root command so the logger sees parsed `--verbose` / `--json` before any subcommand `RunE` fires. Pre-`Execute` construction will not see flag values; post-`Execute` construction is too late.
- Fix the existing dead `verbose=false` on `internal/cli/root.go:26` while in the area — the error-path `Output` should reflect the real flag value.

## Out of scope
- Threading `cli.Deps` into command constructors (`newListCmd`, etc.). Commands are all `ErrNotImplemented` stubs today; each domain epic will plumb `Deps` into its own command when it actually needs the logger.
- Adding any actual log calls. No code in `internal/` logs anything yet; this task is wiring only.

## Specs
- specs/code-standards.md §5 (lines 260–314)
- specs/architecture-principles.md §9
- specs/cli-framework.md §5 (stream separation invariant)

## Acceptance
- `setupLogger(verbose, jsonOutput)` returns a logger matching `specs/code-standards.md` §5: TextHandler vs JSONHandler chosen by `jsonOutput`, level Info or Debug chosen by `verbose`, writing to `os.Stderr`.
- `cli.Deps.Logger` is populated when `cli.Execute()` runs, based on the parsed `--verbose` / `--json` values.
- `cli.NewRoot(cli.Deps{})` (zero-value Deps, as in current tests) does not panic and produces a working command tree backed by a discard logger.
- The error-path `Output` constructed in `cli.Execute()` reflects the real `--verbose` value, not a hardcoded `false`.
- No regression in existing CLI tests.
close_reason: Implemented SetupLogger in internal/cli/logger.go, added Logger field to Deps, wired via PersistentPreRunE, fixed error-path verbose flag. All tests pass, lint clean.

title: Implement structured logging
description:
Set up slog-based structured logging across havn. Currently no logger is wired anywhere — the output helper handles user-facing output but there is no observability layer.

## Scope
- Logger initialization in cmd/havn/main.go (or internal/cli bootstrap)
- slog configuration per specs/code-standards.md §5:
  - Default level INFO; DEBUG when --verbose is set
  - Text handler by default; JSON handler when --json is set
  - Output to stderr (never stdout — keeps the data stream clean per cli-framework.md §5)
- Pass *slog.Logger through cli.Deps so domain packages can log without importing a global
- Standard attribute conventions (component, operation, container_name, error)
- Error logging pattern: log at the boundary that handles the error, not at every wrap site
- Coexist with the output helper (logger → stderr, Status → stderr, Data → stdout)

## Out of scope
- Third-party log shipping / aggregation
- Trace/span propagation (OpenTelemetry)

## Specs
- specs/code-standards.md §5
- specs/architecture-principles.md §9
- specs/cli-framework.md §5 (logger must not pollute stdout)

## Acceptance
- slog.Logger is available via cli.Deps to all commands and domain packages
- --verbose enables DEBUG level
- --json selects the JSON handler
- stdout remains reserved for command data output; all log records go to stderr
- No package uses fmt.Println / log.Printf for diagnostic output

title: Implement doctor checks
description:
Implement internal/doctor/ with all tier 1 and tier 2 health checks plus the three output modes (human, verbose, JSON). Currently havn doctor is wired as a CLI stub returning ErrNotImplemented.

## Scope
- internal/doctor/ package
- Check interface with stable check IDs per spec
- Tier 1 checks (always run, 8 total): 1.1 docker_daemon, 1.2 base_image, 1.3 network, 1.4 volumes, 1.5 global_config, 1.6 project_config, 1.7 dolt_server, 1.8 dolt_database
- Tier 2 checks (running containers only, 7 total): 2.1 nix_store, 2.2 nix_devshell, 2.3 project_mount, 2.4 config_mounts, 2.5 ssh_agent, 2.6 dolt_connectivity, 2.7 beads_health
- Runner that executes checks, collects results, supports prerequisite skip logic
- Three output formatters: human (default), verbose (detail per check), JSON (per spec)
- Exit codes: 0 = all pass, 1 = warnings, 2 = errors (via ExitError)
- Uses internal/docker, internal/config, internal/volume

## Wires up
- havn doctor CLI command — replace ErrNotImplemented stub
- Adds DoctorRunner to cli.Deps

## Specs
- specs/havn-doctor.md
- specs/havn-overview.md (doctor command interface)

## Acceptance
- All 14 checks implemented with stable IDs matching spec §Check Identifiers
- --all flag gates tier 2 checks to all running containers
- Three output modes work and match spec
- Exit codes are correct (0/1/2)

title: Implement Dolt domain
description:
Implement internal/dolt/ plus Docker wrapper extensions for managing the shared Dolt server container and databases. Covers all 8 havn dolt subcommands which are currently ErrNotImplemented stubs.

## Scope
- internal/dolt/ package
- Docker wrapper extensions for dolt-specific container needs
- Operations:
  - StartServer / StopServer / Status (havn dolt start/stop/status)
  - ListDatabases (havn dolt databases)
  - DropDatabase (havn dolt drop, --yes confirmation handled in CLI)
  - OpenShell (havn dolt connect)
  - ImportDatabase (havn dolt import)
  - ExportDatabase (havn dolt export)
- Volume setup for dolt data persistence
- Dolt config file generation
- Beads env var injection for connected containers
- Per-project config parsing for dolt settings
- Uses internal/config, internal/docker, internal/name
- Domain error types

## Wires up
- All 8 dolt CLI commands in internal/cli/dolt.go — replace ErrNotImplemented stubs
- Adds DoltManager to cli.Deps

## Specs
- specs/shared-dolt-server.md
- specs/havn-overview.md (dolt subcommand contracts)

## Acceptance
- All 8 dolt subcommands work end-to-end
- Dolt container lifecycle (start/stop/status) is correct and idempotent
- Import/export round-trip preserves data
- Drop requires --yes and produces clear errors without it
- Beads env var is set correctly on connected containers
- JSON output shapes match spec

title: Implement mount package
description:
Create internal/mount/ for resolving all bind mounts, named volume mounts, and SSH forwarding mounts when starting a havn container. Pure domain logic: takes config + path and produces a complete list of mount specs plus env vars the docker wrapper can consume.

## Scope
- internal/mount/ package
- Resolve(config, projectPath, homeDir, opts) → ResolveResult{Mounts, Env}
- Handles:
  - Project directory bind mount (always present, rw)
  - Config file mounts from mounts.config (path:mode entries, conditional on host file existence)
  - Wildcard mount expansion (.gitconfig-* → all matching files)
  - SSH agent forwarding (mount SSH_AUTH_SOCK socket, set env var)
  - SSH authorized_keys mount
  - Named volume mounts (nix, data, cache, state)
  - Path expansion (~ and env vars)
  - Read-only vs read-write
- Pure function with injected I/O callbacks (Glob, Exists)

## Wires up
- Used by the container package during container create/start
- No direct CLI command binding

## Specs
- specs/havn-overview.md §'Bind mounts' (lines 474-494)
- specs/havn-overview.md §Configuration mounts section (lines 283-299)
- specs/havn-overview.md §'Named volumes' (lines 459-472)
- specs/havn-overview.md §'Wildcard mount support' (lines 491-494)
- specs/architecture-principles.md §1
- specs/code-standards.md §3

## Acceptance
- Deterministic given config + path + injected callbacks
- Wildcard mounts expand correctly
- SSH agent forwarding produces mount + env var when socket exists; omitted when not
- Named volumes produce correct container paths
- Invalid mounts produce typed domain errors

title: Implement volume package
description:
Create internal/volume/ for managing havn-owned Docker volumes. Used by havn volume list and by doctor tier 1.4 checks.

## Scope
- internal/volume/ package
- Operations:
  - List (for havn volume list)
  - EnsureExists (used by container start to create per-project volumes)
  - Remove (if in spec)
- Uses internal/docker wrapper
- Uses internal/name for volume naming
- Domain error types

## Wires up
- CLI command volume list — replace ErrNotImplemented stub
- Adds VolumeManager to cli.Deps

## Specs
- specs/havn-overview.md (volume list)
- specs/havn-doctor.md (tier 1.4 volume checks)
- specs/architecture-principles.md §1

## Acceptance
- havn volume list works and emits JSON per spec
- EnsureExists is used by the container package during start

title: Implement container package
description:
Create internal/container/ with the domain logic for managing havn containers. Core domain used by list, stop, build, and the root start/attach command.

## Scope
- internal/container/ package
- Runtime interface (backed by internal/docker/)
- Operations:
  - List (for havn list)
  - Stop single / Stop all (for havn stop, havn stop --all)
  - Create + Start + Attach (for root command with path arg)
  - Build image (for havn build)
  - Inspect / GetInfo
- Multi-unit operation handling per architecture-principles.md §6 (best-effort, result collection)
- Domain error types (feeds into havn-xx2)
- Uses internal/name for naming and internal/config for settings

## Wires up
- CLI commands list, stop, build, and root — replace ErrNotImplemented stubs
- Adds Runtime to cli.Deps
- First real use of internal/cli/output.go

## Specs
- specs/havn-overview.md (list, stop, build, root commands)
- specs/architecture-principles.md §1, §4, §6
- specs/code-standards.md §2, §4

## Acceptance
- havn list, havn stop, havn stop --all, havn build work end-to-end
- Root command (havn [path]) starts/attaches a container
- JSON output shapes match spec

title: Implement Docker wrapper package
description:
Create internal/docker/ as the sole boundary to the Docker SDK. All higher-level packages (container, volume, dolt, doctor) depend on this wrapper, never on the Docker SDK directly.

This isolates the Docker SDK types from domain code so the SDK version can be upgraded without churning the whole codebase.

## Scope
- internal/docker/ package
- Minimal surface — only what callers actually need:
  - Container: Create, Start, Stop, Remove, List, Inspect, Exec (one-shot + interactive)
  - Volume: Create, List, Inspect
  - Network: Create, List, Inspect
  - Image: Build, Inspect, Exists
  - File copy: CopyToContainer, CopyFromContainer
- Interface(s) defined in this package that higher layers depend on
- Real implementation using Docker SDK
- Translate Docker SDK errors into domain error types (havn-xx2)

## Out of scope
- Dolt-specific wrapper calls (live alongside the dolt domain)
- Any domain logic — this package only does mechanical Docker calls
- Image pull (havn build is local-only per spec)

## Specs
- specs/architecture-principles.md §4
- specs/code-standards.md §4

## Acceptance
- No package outside internal/docker/ imports github.com/docker/docker/*
- Interface surface is small and scoped to actual caller needs
- Docker SDK errors are translated to domain error types

title: Implement name-derivation primitive
description:
Pure-function package to derive container/image/volume/network names from a filesystem path.

Example: ~/Repos/github.com/user/api → havn-user-api

Small but load-bearing primitive used by the root command (start/attach), container package, and doctor checks. No dependencies, no I/O.

## Scope
- internal/name/ package
- Named types: ContainerName, ImageName, VolumeName, NetworkName
- Derivation function(s) from absolute path
- Validation (allowed characters, length limits)

## Specs
- specs/architecture-principles.md §1
- specs/havn-overview.md (naming scheme)
- specs/code-standards.md §3

## Acceptance
- Pure functions, no I/O
- Derivation is deterministic and idempotent where applicable

title: Implement error handling system
description:
Complete the error handling layer: domain error types plus FormatError translation at the CLI boundary.

Currently internal/cli/errors.go:34-36 has a FormatError stub that returns err.Error() unchanged, and no domain error types exist.

## Scope
- Domain error types in their owning packages (not in cli/):
  - container.NotFoundError, container.ImageNotFoundError, container.AlreadyRunningError
  - config.ValidationError, config.NotFoundError
  - docker.DaemonUnreachableError
  - Others as needed per spec examples
- Each type implements error and exposes structured fields for JSON output
- FormatError in internal/cli/errors.go translates domain errors to user-facing messages
- JSON error output uses structured fields (type, code, details)
- ExitError wrapping with correct exit codes per command

## Specs
- specs/code-standards.md §2
- specs/cli-framework.md §6
- specs/architecture-principles.md §5

## Notes
- Error types live in their owning domain package, not in cli/
- This epic will grow as each domain package lands; the base FormatError translation framework and first few error types should land together so subsequent domains have a pattern to copy

## Acceptance
- FormatError produces useful human-readable messages for all defined domain errors
- JSON error output shape matches spec
- Exit codes are correct per command (doctor has 0/1/2, others 0/1)

title: Implement config package
description:
Create internal/config/ package to load, merge, validate, and resolve havn configuration per precedence: flag > env > project (.havn/config.toml) > global (~/.config/havn/config.toml) > default.

## Scope
- Config struct types (global, project, merged)
- TOML load (global + project files)
- Default values
- Merge with precedence
- Validation
- Flag/env overlay
- Wire into cli.Deps

## Out of scope
- Using config values in domain logic (happens in domain epics)

## Specs
- specs/havn-overview.md §8
- specs/architecture-principles.md §8
- specs/code-standards.md §3

## Acceptance
- config.Load(globalPath, projectPath) returns a merged Config
- Precedence behaves correctly (flag > env > project > global > default)
- Invalid config produces domain error types with clear messages
- cli.Deps.Config is populated in Execute()

title: Add version flag with build-time injection
description: Add version support: var version = "dev" in internal/cli/root.go, set root.Version = version. Update Makefile build target to inject version via -ldflags. Verify: 'havn --version' prints 'havn version dev', 'make build' produces binary with dev version. Test: --version flag outputs version string.
close_reason: Version variable and root.Version set. Build-time injection via ldflags supported. Test added and passing.
